markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Kendley Nathrael ](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2141)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The captain of the Eagle Garrison is a skilled, experienced ranger in her own right, but the influence of the _convergence lattice_ imakes her a deadly foe and a threat to the region she's supposed to be monitoring—all in the name of unification.

 Kendley Nathrael was born and raised in a small community in Nirmathas along the southwestern border of the Fangwood. She spent a significant part of her youth evading military incursions from Molthune. She sought out mentorship and opportunities to continue learning over the years, slowing rising through the ranks, informal as they were, of the Nirmathi militia. Her life was dangerous, thrilling, and deeply rewarding.

 Then the [hobgoblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=13) came. The Ironfang Invasion swept indiscriminately across southern Nirmathas and Molthune, razing villages and driving the survivors into the forest. Kendley found herself leading a desperate group of refugees to the Nirmathi capital of Tamran, where the survivors found refuge and Kendley found herself working alongside other militia leaders to respond to the sudden and dire hobgoblin threat.

 Higher-ranking militia members in Nirmathas took note of Kendley's abilities, so when strange reports started trickling down from the Nirmathas-Lastwall border, they assigned Kendley to scout and evaluate the situation. She traveled north along Lake Encarthan and up the Tourondel River, hoping to make a circuit of the nation, but that circuit was abruptly cut short when the Whispering Tyrant obliterated Vigil, Lastwall's capital. Overnight, Kendley went from scouting a region for danger to fleeing from it, once again leading a band of refugees into the Fangwood for safety. For several weeks, she managed to keep her ragtag followers alive in the face of the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) horrors rising up across the devastated nation, and when she stumbled across the Knights of Lastwall making their own way to Absalom, she insisted they take along her group of survivors. To her surprise, those survivors insisted she remain with them; with the region in shambles, she agreed to accompany them to the City at the Center of the World.

 Absalom completely overwhelmed Kendley, and so she sought out the only structure she was familiar with: the military. She enlisted in Absalom's First Watch, and the skills she had built over her years in Nirmathas helped her quickly rise through the ranks. It was only a few months before she was promoted to a captain's post, but despite her success, Kendley still found the city uncomfortable and oppressive; she missed the cool hills and dense forests of her homeland terribly. A few days after mentioning this longing in passing to a few guard colleagues, however, her commanding officer came by with a question: Had Kendley heard of the Eagle Garrison?

 The opportunity seemed too good to be true. A position outside of the confines of Absalom's walls, high in the foothills of the nearby mountains. A role centered around the scouting and reconnaissance work she had excelled at in Nirmathas. Even her command of the [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) language—learned through her interactions with the Fangwood's [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69)—would serve her well communicating with the noble [giant eagles](/Monsters.aspx?ID=173) allied with the Eagle Garrison. Moreover, the garrison's present captain was looking to retire once a suitable replacement had been found. Perhaps, the officer suggested, that role might appeal to her?

 She accepted immediately.

 Captain Nathrael has been posted at the Eagle Garrison for just under 6 months. Though she operates within a larger hierarchy, the bureaucracy is a distant thing and she rarely has to deal with it. The mountains are different from the woodlands of her home, but the work is largely the same, and she's found a kinship with the other rangers similar to what she had with her old colleagues further north. Having lived through several crisis events, she gauges everything with a level head and a keen eye that her subordinates have rapidly come to trust and respect. 

<title level="2" right="">Campaign Role</title> When the heroes first encounter Kendley, she's been incorporated into the _convergence lattice_. The convergent effect identified her as the most capable creature in the hive mind and centered itself on her as the “authority,” drastically augmenting her power. Unfortunately, the lattice's goal of forced unification for all puts her at odds with the heroes, and she does everything she can to incorporate the heroes into the hive mind.

 As with all the Eagle Garrison rangers caught up in the convergence lattice, Kendley is simply being controlled. Killing her certainly lessens the immediate threat the _convergence lattice_ poses, but if the heroes can remove the effect or destroy the lattice without killing her, she regains the behaviors and abilities she had before the lattice empowered her (without the convergent abilities, Kendley is a 12th-level creature). If returned to her own senses, Kendley can serve as an ally and source of reliable information. She's familiar with the region and she knows [Sarvel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2143) and his entourage (consisting of several dozen [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102), over a dozen [saurians](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=204), and several [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28)) passed by the garrison and headed into the Vale of Aroden. She's also heard many rumors about what might be found in the Vale, although she's never seen it herself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 19">[Convergent Kendley Nathrael](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2141)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />

markdown:
A cunning historian and summoner from a line of [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) who retain the psychic might of their fearsome ancestors, Kirosthrek serves [Sarvel Ever-Hunger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2143) in the hopes of gaining the power she needs to restore the xulgaths' ruined Darklands empire. [Thoughtmaw xulgaths](/Monsters.aspx?ID=982) are larger, more intelligent, and more powerful than others of their kind, and Kirosthrek has long embraced that truth. Where many of her kin heard only fanciful legend and exaggerated myth in the tales told by her clutch's elders, those recountings of powerful xulgaths ruling the Darklands resonated with Kirosthrek. Most of her kin feared the spirits of the past and looked only to the present and the future, but Kirosthrek dug into that past, hunting through the ruins of the long-dead xulgath empire around her for fragments of lost lore and forgotten secrets. Bit by bit, Kirosthrek expanded her knowledge. From ancient texts, she began to piece together even more powerful spells and rituals. Others in her clutch came to fear the ancient magic she commanded, and so she found herself with followers willing to do her bidding if she spared their minds. She took her elders' place, telling not mere tales but true histories of their people, of the Darklands vault they once ruled and the abrupt end of that empire. The thoughtmaws were the true descendants of that empire, she insisted, and some day they would rule it again. The isolated clutch she led was too small to retake an empire single-handedly, so Kirosthrek sought allies. This search brought to her attention a high priest of [Zevgavizeb](/Deities.aspx?ID=82) calling himself Sarvel Ever-Hunger, leading a fierce conquest in the upper Darklands. He was a lesser xulgath, but one infused with fiendish might and the blessings of a demon lord, and so Kirosthrek led her clutch to join forces with this new conqueror, hoping to subjugate the priest into furthering her own goals. Sarvel was not easily subjugated, however, and while he welcomed the thoughtmaws' addition to his might, Kirosthrek was forced to adjust her own plans and serve as Sarvel's chief advisor. The priest's interests were not so different from her own, she found; furthermore, he held his own scraps of knowledge about the ancient empire Kirosthrek so admired. From him, she learned that the vault they ruled had been known as Vask, and that some sudden tragedy had darkened the life-giving suns of that realm. She took that name to her daemonic allies, seeking to learn more about that catastrophe, and from them, she learned about Aroden's theft. For Kirosthrek, this revelation was a clear directive: she would go find the sun-orbs Aroden had stolen and reclaim them, using their power to rebuild the empire of old. And for a time, it seemed Sarvel shared her goals. The conquering high priest fixed his sights on the surface world, organizing an operation with enough subtlety and savvy that Kirosthrek began to consider him a suitable leader. But Sarvel's true goal with the orbs is now clear to her: rather than reclaim the orbs, he wants them destroyed. Hoping to make the most of a plan gone wrong, Kirosthrek continues to aid Sarvel, but only from a lack of better options; her opinion of the priest now calling himself the Apocalypse Prophet continues to sour. Campaign Role Kirosthrek is not the heroes' ally, but neither is she an unreasonable zealot. She fights alongside Sarvel and views the heroes as soft-skinned surface dwellers who don't deserve the privileges that have benefited them throughout their lives. But she is a quick thinker and a survivor; if the odds are obviously stacked against her, she doesn't hesitate to prioritize her own self-preservation. She doesn't like the heroes, but she respects their power, and she is no stranger to bartering with powerful beings for their aid. Should the heroes defeat Sarvel while Kirosthrek lives, she loses all interest in the surface campaign. Her first instinct is to flee, but if the heroes offer a truce, she's also willing to negotiate with them. She doesn't limit her opening requests, however: she wants the _aeon orbs_ so she can restore the xulgath empire in the Vault of the Black Desert. She also isn't any kinder or gentler than other xulgaths, and she makes no attempt to hide her intentions of subjugating and enslaving other peoples in the areas she intends to claim (such as the [urdefhans](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=158) and [drow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=37) that now live in what she considers xulgath lands). Even so, the heroes may feel they owe the xulgaths some form of reparations for Aroden's theft millennia ago, and Kirosthrek is willing to entertain reasonable offers to benefit her people, in lieu of the _aeon orbs_. The details and repercussions of such an arrangement are beyond the scope of this adventure. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 50 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 [Kirosthrek](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2142) **Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 88 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), thoughtsense (imprecise) 60 **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +33, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +41, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +38, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +37 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 **Thoughtsense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Kirosthrek senses a creature's mental essence at the listed range. **Items** _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_, _[planar ribbon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=670)_, [true elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91) --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +33 **Will** +36 **HP** 375 **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 20 **Psychogenic Secretions** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. A creature that enters the emanation must attempt a DC 42 Will save. On a failure, the creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute (stupefied 2 and also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to psychogenic secretions for 1 minute.
**Divert Thoughts** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** Kirosthrek attempts a saving throw against a spell; **Effect** Kirosthrek diverts some of the spell's energy, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to the saving throw or +4 if the spell has the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) trait. If she critically succeeds at the saving throw, she can use the Cast a Spell action on her next turn to cast the triggering spell at the same level without expending any spell slots. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet (from [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=125)) **Melee** jaws +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d8+6 piercing plus 4d6 mental **Melee** claw +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d4+6 slashing plus 4d6 mental **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 42 - The creature must attempt a DC 42 Will save. Using Consume Knowledge immediately ends any of the ability's previous effects on a different creature.

markdown:
The fiendish [xulgath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) priest of [Zevgavizeb](/Deities.aspx?ID=82) known as the Apocalypse Prophet is an ambitious, hateful monster fixated solely on bringing death and destruction to the reviled surface-dwellers upon the Isle of Kortos. In a squalid colony nestled deep in the Darklands realm of Nar-Voth, the xulgath Sarvel hatched, first of his clutch to break free from the shell. Sarvel was also the last of that clutch to do so, for upon breaking free of his calcified prison, he promptly crushed the other eggs and devoured their contents in a fit of ravenous fratricide. This gluttonous rampage served him well, for the sustenance helped him grow swiftly among kin who recognized only strength and might. As he grew older, he gorged himself upon fallen foes and allies he found too weak to provide any other benefit. Sarvel became known as a leader to be feared and followed. Though it hadn't been Sarvel's intention to draw the attention of his clutch's priests, they recognized in Sarvel the gifts of their vile god Zevgavizeb. In an ill-conceived effort to bring forth those gifts, they beseeched their patron to bestow further blessings upon him. The [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) who responded feasted upon the startled priests, but Sarvel conquered the demon and consumed it in turn. This meal profoundly changed Sarvel. Perhaps it was the [Abyssal](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) power of the demon, the divine energy carried within the priests' bodies, or a combination of the two. Fiendish power surged within him, and he was seized with a desperate hunger beyond any he had ever known. Sarvel emerged from the priests' sanctum bearing the blessings of Zevgavizeb in the form of writhing tentacles emerging from his back, and he commanded the demon lord's own power through his spells. Few dared oppose him when he declared himself high priest and took the name Ever-Hunger, and those who did became his next screaming meals. For years, Sarvel led his clutch to many conquests in the Darklands, but these victories were never enough. From his communion with Zevgavizeb, he learned that a sacred vault called Vask once existed as a haven for his people. With help from one of his xulgath allies—the thoughtmaw [Kirosthrek](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2142)—he painstakingly reconstructed records of scattered xulgaths from millennia ago, learning of Aroden's theft in the process. A kinder or more reasonable xulgath might have tried to steal the orbs back, or even negotiate for their return, but Sarvel is neither kind nor reasonable. He instead embarked on a mission to destroy what Aroden had wrought and crush those surface-dwellers who dared benefit from the slight to his people. A conquest of deific scale must surely sate his hunger; if not, he shall feast upon the bodies left in its wake. Campaign Role Sarvel Ever-Hunger is the final foe of the Extinction Curse Adventure Path. His machinations threaten the stability of the Starstone Isles. He knows that the heroes are the ones who have foiled each of the champions he placed at the aeon towers, and he harbors a grudge as enormous as his hunger. Sarvel holds no compassion—only hatred and a vicious thirst for destruction fueled by fiendish spite. Granted some degree of power over the _aeon orbs_ by the _extinction curse_ ritual, he is difficult to defeat, and he holds no reservations about slaying the heroes and feasting upon their flesh. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 54 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 54 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 52 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 [Sarvel Ever-Hunger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2143) **Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 90 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +41, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +40, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +42, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +40, [Zevgavizeb Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +40 **Str** +9 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [unholy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=307) [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)_ --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +34 **Will** +36 **HP** 430 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, good 15 **Echoes of Aeons** At the start of Sarvel's turn, if he is dead, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or otherwise unable to act, his efforts in the unfinished extinction curse ritual pull on the power of an _aeon orb_. The surge of power heals his body completely without causing the wounded condition, causes him to grow an additional tentacle, and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) all detrimental effects on Sarvel as a 10th-level effect with a +42 counteract modifier. An _aeon orb_ used by Sarvel or the heroes is depleted and can't be used to power this ability.
**Enraging Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 30 feet. A creature that enters or begins their turn in the emanation must attempt a DC 45 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round; on a critical failure, it can't target Sarvel with any Strikes or other effects from its confused condition. While within the emanation, the creature takes a –2 penalty to saves to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to all [xulgaths'](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) stenches for 1 minute. Sarvel can suppress or resume this aura as a free action.
**Touch of Zevgavizeb** Sarvel begins combat with two tentacles. Each tentacle has 66 HP and is immune to area damage. A creature can attempt to sever one of Sarvel's tentacles by specifically targeting it and dealing damage equal to the tentacle's Hit Points. A tentacle not completely severed returns to full HP at the end of any creature's turn. For each tentacle he has, Sarvel gains an extra reaction at the start of his turn that can be used only for an Attack of Opportunity. If he has no tentacles, he can no longer make tentacle Strikes, Writhe Independently, or Constrict.

It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Drain Corpse** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The ghoul gnawer uses Consume Flesh; **Effect** In addition to gaining Hit Points, using Consume Flesh refreshes one spell slot the cultist has already expended.

**Ghoul Fever**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 3d6 negative damage and regains half as many hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 3d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); Stage 6 dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight

**Paralysis**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living, non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) creature hit by a ghoul gnawer's attack must succeed at a DC 27 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). The creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

**Swift Leap** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghoul gnawer jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Nydazuul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2145) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 25 **Perception** +20; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +20 **HP** 180 **Immunities** [controlled](/Traits.aspx?ID=241), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 10, physical 10 (except [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375)) **Weaknesses** [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) 10 --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** bite +23 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+12 plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), 1d6 chaotic, and rotting curse **Melee** sting +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d4+12 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 1d6 chaotic **Occult Innate Spells** DC 31 - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (×3) - **Constant (7th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Confusing Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The augnagar's writhing limbs and flesh seethe and squirm in a disorienting and unsettling manner. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 30 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Confusing Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature can't attempt a flat check to recover from confusion whenever it takes damage from an attack or spell. **Inhale Vitality** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The augnagar inhales sharply, drawing life force out of creatures in a 50-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 12d6 negative damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save, and the creature is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) on a failure). The augnagar becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round on its next turn, and it can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. **Rotting Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 day); **Stage 2** drained 2 and the creature displays hideous, festering wounds exuding a horrific stench. Any creature within 30 feet of the victim must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) on a critical failure, for as long as it's sickened). While within the aura, affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against disease and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute. The victim of the disease doesn't roll a save, but takes the failure effects automatically and can't reduce its sickened value below 1 (1 day).
name: Nydazuul

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Solar Glass Golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2147) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 40 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +20 **Will** +17 **HP** 195 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), magic (see Golem Antimagic below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) or bludgeoning) **Golem Antimagic** harmed by [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) (8d6, 2d6 from areas or persistent magic); healed by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) (2d6 HP); slowed by [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)
**Vulnerable to Shatter** A glass golem is affected by the [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) spell as though the golem were an unattended object.
**Spell Reflection** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Trigger** The glass golem is targeted by a spell; **Effect** The glass golem positions its magical, reflective surfaces to turn the spell back on the caster. It tries to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell by attempting an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check for its counteract check. If it successfully counteracts the spell, the effect is turned back on the caster. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** stained glass frame +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+13 slashing plus 1d6 bleed **Solar Flare** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) The glass golem holds up its stained-glass frame and angles it downward, causing the image of the sun in the artwork to flash brightly, illuminating a 30-foot cone. This blast of light deals 8d6 fire damage to creatures in the area, plus 4d6 additional positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 30 Reflex save. If this solar flare overlaps an area of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the darkness effect (+22 counteract check).
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) permanently.
name: Solar Glass Golem

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Vampire Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2149) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 50 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Children of the Night** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The vampire's presence brings forth creatures of the night to do the master's bidding. These typically include rat swarms, bat swarms, and wolves, but can include other creatures. The vampire can give telepathic orders to these creatures as long as they are within 100 feet, but they can't communicate back. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)_, _+1 [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows) --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 **HP** 132 (coffin restoration, [fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)) **Weaknesses** [vampire weaknesses](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) **Mist Escape** **Trigger** The vampire is reduced to 0 HP. **Effect** The vampire uses Turn to Mist. It can take [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions to move toward its coffin even though it's at 0 HP. While at 0 HP in this form, the vampire is unaffected by further damage. It automatically returns to its corporeal form, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), if it reaches its coffin or after 2 hours, whichever comes first. --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** _rapier_ +22 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus Grab **Ranged** _shortbow_ +20 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire transforms into one of its animal forms or back into its normal form. Most vampires can turn into a bat, but some can turn into a different creature, such as a rat or a wolf. Use the options in the aerial form and animal form spells as guidelines. A vampire guardian's giant bat form's fangs have a +21 attack bonus and deal 2d6+11 piercing damage.
  • Bat The vampire changes into a bat swarm or giant bat. It gains echolocation 40 feet, a land Speed of 20 feet and a fly Speed of 30 feet, and a fangs Strike with the same attack bonus as its highest melee attack. These fangs deal the same amount of damage as the vampire's claws, but deal piercing damage rather than slashing damage.
**Create Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by Drink Blood, the vampire can turn this victim into a vampire by donating some of its own blood to the victim and burying the victim in earth for 3 nights. If the new vampire is lower level than its creator, it is under the creator's control. If a vampire controls too many spawn at once (as determined by the GM), strong-willed spawn can free themselves by succeeding at a Will saving throw against the vampire's Will DC. **Dominate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The vampire can cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) at will as a divine innate spell (DC 26). Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that vampire's Dominate for 24 hours. Fully destroying the vampire ends the domination, but merely reducing the vampire to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell. **Drink Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the vampire's reach. **Effect** The vampire sinks its fangs into that creature to drink its blood. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the vampire guardian regains 13 HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the vampire but increases the victim's drain value by 1. A vampire can also consume blood that's been emptied into a vessel for sustenance, but it gains no HP from doing so.
A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drain by 1 after 10 minutes. **Turn to Mist** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire turns into a cloud of vapor, as the [_gaseous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) spell, or back to its normal form. The vampire loses [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) while in gaseous form. The vampire can remain in this form indefinitely. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A vampire guardian deals 1d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Vampire Guardian

**3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [_vampiric touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=354)* - **4th** [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)*, [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) (3 slots) - **5th** [Prying Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=239), [_shadow blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=273)*, [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) (3 slots) - **6th** [Mislead](/Spells.aspx?ID=199), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=268), [_vampiric exsanguination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=353)* - **7th** [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Visions of Danger](/Spells.aspx?ID=358) (2 slots) **Bard Composition Spells** DC 33, 2 Focus Points - **Cantrips (7th)** [Inspire Courage](/Spells.aspx?ID=386) - **7th** [Counter Performance](/Spells.aspx?ID=381), [Lingering Composition](/Spells.aspx?ID=389) **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire transforms into one of its animal forms or back into its normal form. Most vampires can turn into a bat, but some can turn into a different creature, such as a rat or a wolf. Use the options in the [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=4) and [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) spells as guidelines. Harmony in Agony's giant bat shape's fangs have a +26 attack bonus and deal 3d8+10 piercing damage.
  • **Bat** The vampire changes into a bat swarm or giant bat. It gains echolocation 40 feet, a land Speed of 20 feet and a fly Speed of 30 feet, and a fangs Strike with the same attack bonus as its highest melee attack. These fangs deal the same amount of damage as the vampire's claws, but deal piercing damage rather than slashing damage.
**Create Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by Drink Blood, the vampire can turn this victim into a vampire by donating some of its own blood to the victim and burying the victim in earth for 3 nights. If the new vampire is lower level than its creator, it is under the creator's control. If a vampire controls too many spawn at once (as determined by the GM), strong-willed spawn can free themselves by succeeding at a Will saving throw against the vampire's Will DC. **Dominate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The vampire can cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) at will as a divine innate spell (DC 30). Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that vampire's Dominate for 24 hours. Fully destroying the vampire ends the domination, but merely reducing the vampire to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell. **Drink Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the vampire's reach. **Effect** The vampire sinks its fangs into that creature to drink its blood. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the vampire regains 16 HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the vampire but increases the victim's drain value by 1. A vampire can also consume blood that's been emptied into a vessel for sustenance, but it gains no HP from doing so. **Signature Spells** Harmony in Agony can freely heighten her signature spells (noted in her spell list above by asterisks. **Turn to Mist** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire turns into a cloud of vapor, as the [_gaseous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) spell, or back to its normal form. The vampire loses fast healing while in gaseous form. The vampire can remain in this form indefinitely.
name: Harmony In Agony

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Ancient Skaveling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2151) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 63 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), echolocation 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +4 **Echolocation** The ancient skaveling can use its hearing as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense at the listed range. --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +27 **Will** +29 **HP** 270 --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** fangs +29 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d12+16 piercing plus ghoul fever and paralysis **Melee** wing +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus paralysis **Bone-Shattering Screech** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The ancient skaveling unleashes a devastating screech that not only chills the bones but shatters them. The screech can be heard throughout the region and alerts all creatures in the Hanging Castle. Creatures in a 20-foot emanation take 12d6 sonic damage and must attempt a DC 36 Will save. The ancient skaveling can't use Bone-Shattering Screech again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Bone-Shattering Screech for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is frightened 2
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is frightened 3, and is [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). **Ghoul Fever** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** DC 36 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 2d6 negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 2d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight **Paralysis** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Any creature hit by the skaveling's Strikes must attempt a DC 36 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
**Failure** The creature is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each save.
name: Ancient Skaveling

During the war between Geb and Nex, necromancers conducted dangerous magical experiments to gain every possible advantage over their enemies. One such endeavor attempted to distill the essence of vampiric mists to create a protective shroud for an army of undead maguses. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Bloodshroud](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2152) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 82 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +5 **Dex** +8 **Con** +2 **Int** +5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_ --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +26 **Will** +23 **HP** 210 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10, piercing 10, slashing 10 **Sanguine Shroud** ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The bloodshroud is enveloped by a shroud of flowing blood. If a creature deals damage to a bloodshroud with a melee Strike or touches the bloodshroud, the creature must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. Any temporary Hit Points the bloodshroud gains from Sanguine Shroud fade after 1 hour.
**Critical Success** As success, but the creature becomes temporarily immune to Sanguine Shroud for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The bloodshroud gains 15 temporary Hit Points.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature becomes [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), and the bloodshroud gains 25 temporary Hit Points.
**Bloodshift** ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of the bloodshroud takes damage; **Effect** The bloodshroud transports itself and any items it's carrying to any unoccupied space adjacent to the triggering creature. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d10+15 slashing **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **6th** [Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2) (×3), [Chromatic Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=883) (×3), [Scorching Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=992) (×3), [Shocking Grasp](/Spells.aspx?ID=283) (×3) - **7th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) - **8th** [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224) (×3) **Spellstrike** **Frequency** until recharged; **Effect** The bloodshroud channels a spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires a spell attack roll into a claw or greatsword Strike. The effects of the spell don't occur immediately but are imbued into the bloodshroud's attack instead. The bloodshroud makes a melee Strike with a weapon or claw. The spell is coupled with the bloodshroud's attack, using the attack roll result to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for the purposes of determining the bloodshroud's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty is not applied until after the Spellstrike. Using Bloodshift automatically recharges the bloodshroud's Spellstrike.
Blood-Borne Protectors [Vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97), [liches](/Monsters.aspx?ID=282), [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30), and other powerful evil creatures throughout Golarion might attempt to bind bloodshrouds to their will, perhaps by promising to reverse the magical misfortune that stripped them of their flesh and encased them in permanent shrouds of blood. However, whether such creatures could deliver such a prize remains to be seen.
name: Bloodshroud

<title level="1" pfs="">[Urglid (Grave Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2153)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The hulking monstrosities known as urglids form from the souls of murderous undertakers, sadists who buried their victims alive, and intentionally neglectful grave keepers who gave up their watch over the dead. Standing over 30 feet tall and weighing over 3,000 pounds, the demon's head seems sunken into its torso, and a gaping, toothy mouth opens at the top of its chest.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Urglid.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Urglid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2153)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 83

**Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Abyss Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** +4

**Con** +8

**Int** +4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Consecration Vulnerability** Dedicated to the desecration of graves, an urglid takes 3d6+6 mental damage each round it's within the area of an effect with the [consecration](/Traits.aspx?ID=34) trait. In addition, the demon takes triple the damage from [_holy water_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +20 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 290

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10, good 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 40 feet, climb 20 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 slashing damage

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d12+21 bludgeoning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 30
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) (at will)
- **5th**
[Passwall](/Spells.aspx?ID=216) (at will), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) (×3)
- **8th**
[Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95)
- **Constant (6th)**
[True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Divine Rituals** DC 32

- **1st**
[Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21)

**Earth Glide**   The urglid can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through any earthen matter, including rock. When they do so, the urglid moves at their full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing unless they choose to do so.

**Gravechoke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) The urglid emits a putrid pulse that targets all living creatures within a 30-foot emanation. Creatures in this area that fail a DC 30 Fortitude save become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure).

**Ravenous Earth** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) With a single, devious thought, the urglid causes a mound of grave soil to well up at a creature's feet. A creature failing a DC 30 Reflex save is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33). The restrained creature then begins sinking below the ground into a spontaneously formed grave. A creature restrained by this ability for three rounds is buried 6 feet deep in the ground and begins [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) within 1 minute. A buried creature must be dug up to be freed (see [Burial](/Rules.aspx?ID=647)). A creature who is slain by Ravenous Earth rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Kabriri's Excavators</title>

While many loathe urglids for their ravenous appetite for burial and wanton destruction, priests of [Kabriri](/Deities.aspx?ID=76) insist that the demon lord blessed and oversaw their creation, trusting the fiends with digging the labyrinthine network of tunnels that connect Everglut with the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). It's no surprise, then, that where there's an urglid, there's possibly a pathway to the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13)— and plenty of [ghouls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=218).
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-28" />
name: Urglid

The church of [Kabriri](/Deities.aspx?ID=76) selects only the most devoted and deadly ghouls to protect their libraries of secret-filled tomes. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Ghoul Antipaladin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2154) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 84 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Kabriri Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=19), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), [Heavy Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=70) (20 bolts), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 29 (31 with shield raised) **Fort** +21 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 **HP** 155 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Destructive Vengeance** **Trigger** The ghoul antipaladin takes damage from an enemy within 15 feet; **Effect** The ghoul antipaladin increases the amount of damage they take by 3d6, and they deal 3d6 damage to the triggering enemy, choosing evil or negative damage to deal to the enemy each time the antipaladin uses this reaction. In addition, the antipaladin deals 4 extra damage of the chosen type with their Strikes against the triggering creature until the end of the antipaladin's turn. An enemy damaged from the ghoul antipaladin's Destructive Vengeance also takes 4 [persistent evil damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); this applies only to the damage the reaction itself deals to the triggering creature, not the damage the antipaladin deals with subsequent Strikes.
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** jaws +19, **Damage** 2d8+11 piercing plus ghoul fever and paralysis **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+9 slashing plus paralysis **Melee** flail +19 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning **Ranged** heavy crossbow +19 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 2](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d8+11 piercing **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 25, 1 Focus Point - **5th** [Touch of Corruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=819) **Consume Flesh** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ghoul antipaladin is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour; **Effect** The ghoul antipaladin devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 4d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once. **Ghoul Fever** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 27; **Stage 1** carrier with no effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 3d6 negative damage and regains half as many hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 3d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight **Paralysis** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living, non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) creature hit by a ghoul antipaladin's attack must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). The creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save. **Swift Leap** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghoul antipaladin jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.
name: Ghoul Antipaladin

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ghoul Razorclaw](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2155)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ghoul assassins who survive dipping their claws in molten [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) transform into razorclaws.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ghoul_GhoulRazorclaw.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Ghoul Razorclaw](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2155)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Ghoul" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=218" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 85

**Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +27, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +7

**Con** +2

**Int** +5

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shadow Essence](/Equipment.aspx?ID=127) (3), [infiltrator thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +23 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 230
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Deny Advantage** The razorclaw isn't flat-footed to creatures of 13th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), flanking, or using surprise attack.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +27 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 piercing plus ghoul fever and paralysis

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 3d6+12 slashing plus adamantine edges

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_composite shortbow_ +27 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 2d6+10 piercing

**Adamantine Edges**   The ghoul's claws have been dipped in [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374). The razorclaw treats any object they hit with a claw Strike as if it had half as much Hardness and any creature they hit as if they had half as much physical resistance.

**Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ghoul razorclaw is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour; **Effect** The ghoul razorclaw devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 7d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Ghoul Fever**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 31; **Stage 1** carrier with no effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 4d6 negative damage and regains half as many hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 4d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight.

**Paralysis**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living, non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) creature hit by a ghoul razorclaw's attack must succeed at a DC 31 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). The creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The ghoul razorclaw deals an extra 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

**Surprise Attacker**   On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the ghoul razorclaw.

**Swift Leap** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghoul razorclaw jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Fists Full of Gold</title>

While little is known about origin of the order of ghoul assassins who dip their claws into molten [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271), survivors of razorclaw assassination attempts often find themselves with a lucrative opportunity should they be able to extract the precious metal from the claws of their would-be killers. A player character who uses an adamantine weapon or cutting tool to remove the adamantine and succeeds at a DC 35 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check for each claw (up to 10) can remove 50 gp of adamantine, which can then be sold or [Crafted](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) into adamantine weapons or armor. 
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-52" />
name: Ghoul Razorclaw

<title level="1" pfs="">[Pyrogeist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2156)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Born from the deaths of arsonists who died in the fires they started, pyrogeists are roiling infernos whose spectral fire emits no actual heat and harms only living creatures. Obsessed with sharing their torment with the living, pyrogeists haunt the area of their demise until whatever wrong they refuse to forgive has been righted, and they are finally put to rest. While pyrogeists can't set their own fires, they often use their terrifying illusory capabilities and telekinetic powers to lure their victims into setting themselves ablaze.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 29

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Pyrogeist.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Pyrogeist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2156)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=222" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=149" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 86

**Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +7

**Con** +0

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +18 

+1 status to all saves vs. [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 130
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), precision, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or positive, double resistance against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), channeling resistance (see below)

**Rejuvenation** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a pyrogeist is destroyed, it reforms where it was destroyed after 2d4 days, fully healed. The pyrogeist can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghastly flames +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d12+8 negative

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177)
- **2nd**
[Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (at will)
- **5th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) (at will), [Telekinetic Haul](/Spells.aspx?ID=332) (at will)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) A pyrogeist can spit a cone of spectral fire in a 30-foot cone, dealing 10d6 negative damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature that fails the save also takes 1d6 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is subject to the effects of [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107) until the persistent damage ends.

**Pyrokinetic Whirlwind** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127)) The pyrogeist transforms into a 40-foot tall vortex of spectral flame that appears to immolate everything within a 10-foot burst. A creature within this area take 2d12+20 negative damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save).

**Soul Consumption**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The spirit of a creature that dies from damage dealt by the pyrogeist's Pyrokinetic Whirlwind is trapped within the pyrogeist as if subjected to the effects of the [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21) spell. This effect persists until the pyrogeist is destroyed or chooses to set the soul free. The spirit remains trapped whether or not the pyrogeist is using Pyrokinetic Whirlwind. A pyrogeist can contain only one soul at a time in this manner.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Extinguishing the Spectral Flames</title>

Putting a pyrogeist to rest permanently often requires a ritualist to perform burial rites with at least some fragment of the pyrogeist's mortal remains. Because what's left of a pyrogeist's living form has most likely melted beyond recognition, it can often be difficult to locate. Pyrogeists can sense innately when what lingers of their mortal forms has been disturbed, and often attack without provocation to quell the threat. When a pyrogeist is finally laid to rest and the appropriate sacraments have been performed, the pyrogeist fails to return to and no longer terrorizes the site of its death.
</aside>
name: Pyrogeist

markdown:
Disgusting and dangerous, rotbombers bound around on all four limbs, ripping off the pressurized and explosive necrotic pustules that grow out of their backs to lob them toward their enemies. When they explode, the pustules emit concussive blasts of force and deadly pestilence that can infect a victim. Though slow and overburdened by the intense weight of the pustules, rotbombers can move with a surprising speed when they need to hunt down their quarry. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Rotbomber](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2157) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 87 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -3 **Slow** A rotbomber is permanently [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and can't use reactions. --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +25 **Will** +19 **HP** 250 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 10, slashing 10 **Pressurized Pustules** When a rotbomber takes acid, fire, physical or sonic damage, there is a chance a prematurely developed pustule bursts open, determined by a DC 11 flat check. All creatures in a 10-foot emanation must succeed at DC 29 Fortitude save or become [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). A creature that gets a critical failure becomes sickened 3. Any creature who attempts this save becomes temporarily immune to pressurized pustules for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** jaws +24, **Damage** 3d8+12 piercing plus necrotic rot **Melee** fist +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** necrotic pustule +26 ([Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150)), **Damage** 3d10+14 bludgeoning and negative damage plus necrotic rot **Gaseous Smash** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The rotbomber pulls two necrotic pustules from its body, one in each hand, and leaps into the air in an attempt to detonate them simultaneously on a hapless victim. The rotbomber [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) up to 15 feet horizontally and 10 feet vertically to land in a square adjacent to a target creature and makes a necrotic pustule Strike against the target. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 4d6 bludgeoning damage to the target. This counts as two attacks for the purposes of determining the rotbomber's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty does not increase until after the Gaseous Smash. **Necrotic Rot** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) Necrotic rot withers and corrupts living tissue at an alarming rate. An infected creature can't heal damage it takes from necrotic rot until it has been cured of the disease. **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Stage 1** 2d6 negative damage (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10); **Stage 3** 3d6 negative damage and drained 2; **Stage 4** dead, rising as a rotbomber immediately. **Propulsive Pestilence** The pressurized necrotic gases within the rotbomber's pustules make them explode far more dramatically than most alchemists' bombs. The rotbomber's necrotic pustules deal [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage to every creature within 15 feet of the target.
Rotbomber Variants **Warning - this sidebar contains spoilers for the Blood Lords adventure path.**
Though the stat block presented for the rotbomber represents the most common version of the creature, GMs can feel free to experiment within the paradigms of the campaign to produce their own variants if they wish. For example, the Church of [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) magically treats the [shadow ash](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1723) discovered by the player characters in chapter three to create frostbombers, whose necrotic pustules deal cold damage instead of negative damage. Such rotbomber variants can easily be made to harm foes with fire, electricity, sonic vibrations, or any other damage type the GM deems appropriate.
name: Rotbomber
search_markdown:
<traits>
