<title level="1" pfs="">[Straugh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2158)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When skirmishes break out between tunneling [ghouls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) and creatures that dwell beneath the surface of Golarion, there's a chance that ghoul fever could infect such animals, producing strange new variants of ghoul beasts. However, letting the natural corruption run its course sometimes results in unsatisfying monsters, so some necromancers speed the process. This is the situation that led to the creation of the first straugh. A priest of [Kabriri](/Deities.aspx?ID=76) made the monstrosity to defend a claimed cemetery from another sect of ghouls who were preying upon the interred bodies. In some places, ghouls use straughs to tunnel into rival warrens or highly fortified mausoleums.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Straugh.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Straugh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2158)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Ghoul" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=218" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 88

**Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +3

**Con** +7

**Int** -4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +23,
**Damage** 2d12+12 piercing plus ghoul fever and paralysis

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
talon +23, ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) and paralysis

**Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The straugh is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour; **Effect** The straugh devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 7d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Fetid Screech** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) Unaware that its vocal organs have withered and decayed, the straugh instinctively but ineffectively attempts to screech, instead releasing malodorous gas, half-digested corpse flesh, and small swarms of maggots from its open beak. The straugh releases a disgusting cloud of gas and decaying detritus in a 15-foot cone; any creature within the area must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). On a critical failure, the creature also takes a –5 foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. Any creature that succeeds at the save is temporarily immune to Fetid Screech for 24 hours.

**Ghoul Fever**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 28; **Stage 1** carrier with no effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 4d8 negative damage and regains half as many hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 4d8 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight

**Paralysis**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living, non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) creature hit by a ghoul antipaladin's attack must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). The creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

**Rock Tunneler**   A straugh can burrow through solid stone at a Speed of 10 feet. It can leave a tunnel if it desires, and it usually does.

**Tunneling Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) **Requirements** The straugh has [Burrowed](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) underground and remains [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by the creature it plans to attack; **Effect** The straugh Burrows twice to emerge from the ground adjacent to at least one enemy and immediately makes a Strike against that foe.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Hungry Beasts</title>

Some creatures are especially engineered by foul necromancers to have traits like that of ghouls. [Skavelings](/Monsters.aspx?ID=803) and straughs are examples of this. As a GM you can create your own ghoul variants if you wish. _Pathfinder Book of the Dead_ (see [Undead Adjustments](/Rules.aspx?ID=1700)) provides advice on how to accomplish this on the fly with nearly any creature, though some make more sense than others. The best creatures to receive the ghoul treatment are those normally associated with being ravenous and dangerous. When you want a creature that is significantly more powerful than the base creature, it's sometimes best to be your own necromancer and design a ghoul-like undead beast from the ground up to reflect their increased threat level.
</aside>
name: Straugh

<title level="1" pfs="">[Terrorguard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2159)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Spellcasters who harness the power of the [Dimension of Dreams](/Domains.aspx?ID=9) may use it to fuel vicious servitors known as terrorguards—hulking terrors adorned with frames made of an [Abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405) alloy. Terrorguards come in many forms; creators often design the constructs' frames to mimic grotesque aberrations by mixing-and-matching parts.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 37

**[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Terrorguard.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Terrorguard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2159)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
<trait label="Dream" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=299" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 89

**Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +5

**Con** +8

**Int** -5

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 37 

**Fort** +28 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 255

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dreamscythe +29 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 3d10+18 slashing plus nightmare essence

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +29,
**Damage** 3d10+18 bludgeoning

**Breath of Phantasms** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The terrorguard's ram-skull head opens at the jaw, allowing a horde of swirling phantasms to escape from its maw in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area is subjected to a 5th-level [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) spell (DC 31).

**Draw the Line** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A terrorguard plunges its dreamscythe into the ground and rends a glowing line across the surface that reverberates with the essence of the [Dimension of Dreams](/Domains.aspx?ID=9). Chose up to four unoccupied squares which are adjacent to one another and adjacent to the terrorguard. For 1d4 rounds, any creature besides the terrorguard who enters such a square must succeed at a DC 31 Will save or become [stunned 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) as they behold grotesque visions from the nightmare realm. A creature that succeeds at this save is temporarily immune to the effect for 24 hours.

**Nightmare Essence**  ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature damaged by a terrorguard's dreamscythe hallucinates that the environment and creatures around them have transformed into a hellscape reminiscent of their most horrifying dreams. A creature hit with a dreamscythe Strike must succeed at a DC 31 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round. A creature who succeeds at this save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

**Skull Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The terrorguard Strides twice in a straight line and then makes a horn Strike with a +1 circumstance bonus to its attack roll.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Blades of Terror</title>

A terrorguard's dreamscythes have been carefully forged in the darkest places in the [Dimension of Dreams](/Domains.aspx?ID=9), causing anyone cut by the blade to see the world around them as if they were trapped in their own worst nightmare. As such, some sinister artisans attempt to repurpose dreamscythe blades as components of new magic weapons. A character who spends two hours and succeeds at a DC 27 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check can carefully extract the blade without harming it. Should a character fail, the blade crumbles into a choking dust, a swarm of harmless but disgusting insects, or dissolves into an oily fluid. Salvaged dreamscythes are treated as a [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) and do not carry along the terrorguard's nightmare essence ability. 
</aside>
name: Terrorguard

Hyrune had been a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) for well over a century before he finally caught Geb's attention by exposing a sect of Nexian spies. Geb elevated him to the role of Blood Lord, stationed him atop one of the alcazars, and awarded him an additional holding—a hidden fortress below Mechitar called the Hanging Castle. Hyrune's investigation of this abandoned ruin is where he first came into contact with images of the Great Old One Orgesh, and the strange visions and inspirations he felt tested his faith in [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19). He's come to believe that somewhere between Urgathoa and Orgesh there exists a shadowy amalgamation of the two gods of gluttony. Campaign Role
Hyrune is this adventure's primary antagonist, and as such he's not expected to survive. If he does manage to escape, he could flee to Kemnebi's side and face the characters again. Once the characters learn that Hyrune has taken an interest in them, they might wish to Research him (see [Research](/Rules.aspx?ID=1205)). Each of these in-depth Research checks take 4 hours of downtime to attempt. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Hyrune Loxenna](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2160) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 90 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Ancient Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=101), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Children of the Deep** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Hyrune's presence brings forth creatures of the deep to do his bidding. These typically include [bat swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=45), giant spiders, and [giant centipede](/Monsters.aspx?ID=77), but can include other Darklands creatures. Hyrune can give telepathic orders to these creatures as long as they are within 100 feet, but they can't communicate back. **Items** _Hungerfang_ (_+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [spell-storing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=305) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_; contains [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) at start of combat), _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_ --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +25 **Will** +29 **HP** 240 (coffin restoration, [fast healing 15](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277)) **Weaknesses** [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) **Divided Faith** Hyrune's devotion to [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) has become undermined by his obsession with Orgesh. Urgathoa's religious symbol can be brandished at Hyrune to trigger his revulsion, provided the person attempting to do so accuses Hyrune of being a heretic or of betraying his faith. Hyrune takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Will saves to overcome this revulsion.
**Mist Escape** **Trigger** The vampire is reduced to 0 HP. **Effect** The vampire uses Turn to Mist. It can take move actions to move toward its coffin even though it's at 0 HP. While at 0 HP in this form, the vampire is unaffected by further damage. It automatically returns to its corporeal form, unconscious, if it reaches its coffin or after 2 hours, whichever comes first. --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** _spear_ +31 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d6+16 piercing **Melee** fangs +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d6+16 piercing **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Ranged** _spear_ +26 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d6+16 piercing **Occult Prepared Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) - **2nd** [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), [Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=308) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **4th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254) - **7th** [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237), [Reverse Gravity](/Spells.aspx?ID=261), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **8th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187) **Witch Hexes** DC 36, attack +28, 3 Focus Points - **Cantrips (8th)** [Shroud of Night](/Spells.aspx?ID=806) - **8th** [Blood Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=791), [Malicious Shadow](/Spells.aspx?ID=800), [Phase Familiar](/Spells.aspx?ID=804) **Witch Patron** [night](/Patrons.aspx?ID=5) (Orgesh) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire transforms into one of its animal forms or back into its normal form. Most vampires can turn into a bat, but some can turn into a different creature, such as a rat or a wolf. Use the options in the [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=4) and [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) spells as guidelines. Hyrune's giant bat shape's fangs have a +28 attack bonus and deal 3d12+16 piercing damage
  • **Bat** The vampire changes into a bat swarm or giant bat. It gains echolocation 40 feet, a land Speed of 20 feet and a fly Speed of 30 feet, and a fangs Strike with the same attack bonus as its highest melee attack. These fangs deal the same amount of damage as the vampire's claws, but deal piercing damage rather than slashing damage.
**Create Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by Drink Blood, the vampire can turn this victim into a vampire by donating some of its own blood to the victim and burying the victim in earth for 3 nights. If the new vampire is lower level than its creator, it is under the creator's control. If a vampire controls too many spawn at once (as determined by the GM), strong-willed spawn can free themselves by succeeding at a Will saving throw against the vampire's Will DC. **Dominate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The vampire can cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) at will as a divine innate spell (DC 36). Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that vampire's Dominate for 24 hours. Fully destroying the vampire ends the domination, but merely reducing the vampire to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell. **Drink Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the vampire's reach. **Effect** The vampire sinks its fangs into that creature to drink its blood. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and Hyrune regains 22 HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the vampire but increases the victim's drain value by 1. A vampire can also consume blood that's been emptied into a vessel for sustenance, but it gains no HP from doing so.
A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drain by 1 after 10 minutes. **Familiar** Hyrune's familiar is Taulzhalotel, a one-eyed, long-tailed hairless bat with a fly speed of 25 feet, [darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=5), [manual dexterity](/Familiars.aspx?ID=10) (with its tail), [speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=12), and [spell delivery](/Familiars.aspx?ID=18). **Gluttonous Gulp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Hyrune dislocates his jaw to open his mouth far wider than it should, growing a thicket of additional fangs as he does. He attempts a fangs Strike and deals an additional 3d6 piercing damage on a successful hit and can attempt to Drink Blood if he as an action remaining, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus on his [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to do so. **Turn to Mist** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire turns into a cloud of vapor, as the [_gaseous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) spell, or back to its normal form. The vampire loses fast healing while in gaseous form. The vampire can remain in this form indefinitely.
name: Hyrune Loxenna

([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 5 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 plus ghoul fever and paralysis

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d4+7 plus paralysis

**Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ghoul is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour. **Effect** The ghoul devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 3d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Ghoul Fever**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 22; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 2d6 negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 2d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) the next midnight.

**Hungry Rage** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** Hymmir damages a living creature with his jaws Strike; **Effect** The taste of flesh and blood throws Hymmir into a violent rage. He gains 9 temporary Hit Points, deals an additional 2 damage with melee Strikes, and takes a –1 penalty to AC for 1 minute.

**Gnaw and Chew** <actions string="Three Actions" />  **Requirements** Hymmir is in a hungry rage; **Effect** Hymmir attempts a jaws Strike. If he hits, he chews at his target and inflicts an additional 1d6 slashing damage. Hymmir regains 3d6 Hit Points.

**Paralysis**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Any living, non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) creature hit by a ghoul's attack must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

**Swift Leap** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghoul jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

</column>
name: Hymmir Urath

**Rise Again** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the Disciples are reduced to 0 Hit Points by means other than fire or positive damage, they return to unlife at the start of their next turn. They have 45 Hit Points and are [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in the space in which they were destroyed. The Disciples can't be returned by this ability again for 1 hour, and if destroyed before this time passes, they remain truly dead.

Creatures within a 30-foot emanation take 6d8 mental damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). The Disciples can't use Agonized Howl again for 1d4 rounds.

**Awful Approach** <actions string="Single Action" />  The Disciples reshape their grotesque form to move swiftly. They Stride twice. Any living creature that can see the Disciples during this movement must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). This is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) effect. The Disciples can't use Awful Approach again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Drazmorg the Damned](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2174) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 92 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Items** [_Drazmorg's Staff of All-Sight_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1728), _[In the Shadows of Toil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1731)_ --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +15 **Will** +17 **HP** 115 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Final Spite** **Trigger** The wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points. **Effect** The wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. It doesn't gain any temporary HP from drain life on this Strike. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** claw +15, **Damage** 2d6+4 plus drain life **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Spectral Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=295) - **3rd** [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141) **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the wight damages a living creature with its claw Strike, the wight gains 3 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the wraith increases the amount of drain by 1 on a failed save to a maximum of drained 4. **Raise Bone Wall** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** Drazmorg is in the Lower Vault of Droskar's Crucible; **Effect** Drazmorg draws upon the necromantic energy that infuses the area and causes a tangled wall of bones to rise from the ground. The wall is 5 feet thick, 10 feet wide, and 10 feet tall; once created, it's permanent until Drazmorg uses this ability again, whereupon a previously created bone wall (such as the one at area G2) crumbles. The bone wall has AC 10, Hardness 10, and 25 Hit Points. It's immune to critical hits and precision damage, and it heals damage to itself at a rate of 2d6 Hit Points at the end of a round. Everything on each side of the wall has [greater cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) from creatures on the opposite side, and the wall can't be moved through. A destroyed section of the wall of bones can be moved through freely and no longer heals damage. A creature caught in a space when a bone wall is created must attempt a DC 25 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and the bone wall fails to form in any square occupied by that creature.
**Success** The creature is forced into an adjacent square of its choice as the bone wall forms in its previous space.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature also takes 4d6 piercing and 4d6 bludgeoning damage from the force of being struck by the bone wall. **Wight Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) slain by a wight's claw Strike rises as a [wight](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=163) after 1d4 rounds. This wight spawn is under the command of the wight that killed it. It doesn't have Drain Life or Wight Spawn and becomes [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for as long as it is a wight spawn. If the creator of the wight spawn dies, the wight spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous wight; it regains its free will, gains Drain Life and Wight Spawn, and is no longer clumsy.
name: Drazmorg the Damned

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Dismemberment Table](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2175) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 104 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 22 (18 when broken, construct armor) **Fort** +13 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 56 **Hardness** 8 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 8 **Construct Armor** Like normal objects, an animated object has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated object is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks and its Armor Class is reduced to 18.
**Haunted** The dismemberment table is powered by unquiet spirits, and it takes damage from positive energy as if it were [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). Positive energy bypasses the table's hardness. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** blade +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Melee** strap +14, **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Disabling Strike** The dismemberment table attempts a blade Strike on a single target that it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33). If this Strike hits, the blade cuts deep into one of the target's limbs. The target must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature suffers no additional effect.
**Success** The creature takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Failure** The creature takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage and is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). This clumsy condition ends once the creature is restored to full Hit Points.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but clumsy 2.
name: Dismemberment Table

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Darkmantle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2180)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These dark blue or black mollusk-like creatures have a similar appearance to an octopus, save that they have only six tentacles, all of which are connected by a thick leathery shroud. While darkmantle aren't a particularly good climber, they excel at clinging and can clutch onto a cave roof for days, patiently waiting to ambush any prey that walks below them with a disorienting blob of darkness. When clinging to a cave roof, and with their bulbous red eyes closed down to squints, darkmantles in ambush look similar to stalactites. Their preferred hunting method works best on creatures without darkvision, so they frequent ruins aboveground or shallow caves not far from the outside world. When darkmantles sense prey, they shed darkness down onto their prey before dropping away from the ceiling to flap quickly down to their victim, whereupon these beasts clutch and squeeze with their thick, muscular tentacles. Despite the shape of darkmantles, their bodies's texture is more like thick leather or even rubber—they can't cause damage with their spike-shaped bodies by dropping down onto other creatures.

 Darkmantles have a swift life cycle—their young grow to maturity in a matter of months, and most of these creatures die of old age after living for only a few years. As a result, generations of darkmantles quickly accrue, and over the years, they've developed a similarly rapid propensity to adapt to new locations. Aquatic caverns, for example, might be infested with darkmantles that can swim instead of fly; on the other hand, icy caverns would've led to darkmantles that developed white or pale blue colorations, and these specimens might produce blasts of obscuring fog instead of darkness. The deepest, darkest reaches of the Darklands are rumored to host darkmantles of incredible size, capable of smothering multiple human-sized victims simultaneously. These deep-dwelling darkmantles have the capacity to shed more powerful darkness that can plunge even creatures with darkvision into inky blackness— darkness in which these deep-dwelling creatures can, of course, still see well within.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

tentacles +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8+1 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d8 bludgeoning, DC 17

**Shed Darkness** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The darkmantle sheds an inky blob of black fluid from the folds of its body. It can shed this blob horizontally to a distance of up to 30 feet, or simply let it fall up to 120 feet below itself (shed darkness that falls more than 120 feet before striking a surface evaporates without effect). When the blob of darkness lands, it explodes into a 10-foot burst of darkness that prevents light from penetrating or emanating within the area. Light doesn't enter this area, and any non-magical light sources, such as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) or [lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29), don't emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) of 1st level or lower but has no effect on magical light from 2nd-level or higher spells. From the outside, it appears as a globe of pure darkness. The darkmantle can't shed darkness again for 24 hours.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Frustrating Sheddings</title>

The liquid darkness shed by darkmantles has long fascinated alchemists, who have tried countless methods of extracting and preserving the fluid to generate dark-spewing bombs of their own design. While some alchemists have succeeded in their attempts, the cost and trouble of doing so tend to make the process a great way for an aspiring alchemist to lose a lot of money and waste a lot of time.
</aside>
name: Darkmantle

markdown:
Evil [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) who die having failed to live up to the exacting standards of the [duergar](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=38) god [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28) are sometimes forced to return to the material world as undead abominations known as forge-spurned. Forge-spurned look like dwarves with pupilless eyes and leathery flesh, and with their hair and beard made of smoke and fire—newly formed forge-spurned can almost be mistaken for living creatures as a result. The longer forge-spurned exist, the more skeletal and fiery their visage becomes; those who have existed for centuries or more often look as if they have skulls for faces and fire for hair. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Forge-Spurned](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2181) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 119, [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 83 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +14, [Droskar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** [_soul chain_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1737), [warhammer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=24) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +9 **Will** +11 **HP** 75 (eternal damnation, [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 5 **Eternal Damnation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) As long as their _soul chain_ remains intact, a forge-spurned can't be truly destroyed. They rise again fully healed at the next sundown, even if their physical body was destroyed.
**Searing Heat** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) A creature who touches a forge-spurned (including by [Grappling](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) them or hitting them with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack) takes 2d6 fire damage with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _soul chain_ +15 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** warhammer +14 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 1d8+7 bludgeoning **Inflate Bellows** **Requirements** The forge-spurned has used Forge Breath; **Effect** The forge-spurned draws a massive breath to refill their emptied lungs, enabling them to use Forge Breath again. **Forge Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The forge-spurned breathes a cloud of stinging soot, ash, and glowing embers. This can affect either a 30-foot cone or a 20-foot burst centered on the forge-spurned and persists for 1d4 rounds. Each creature that moves into or starts their turn in the area takes 6d6 fire damage, with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature who fails its save is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute. Creatures within the cloud are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), though not from the forge-spurned. The forge-spurned can't use Forge Breath again until they've used Inflate Bellows. **Soul Chain** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If a creature is slain by an attack from the _soul chain_ and the forge-spurned is able to retain the creature's corpse, they can bind the creature's soul into a _soul chain_ (as [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21)) with a day of work at a forge and a successful DC 20 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check. An individual whose soul is bound in this way can't be resurrected unless the _soul chain_ is destroyed (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10).
Haunted Forges Forge-spurned are often encountered near abandoned forges, and the presence of these undead dwarves can infest that forge with an ever-burning fire. Such “haunted” forges burn without the need for fuel, though they extinguish automatically when their forge-spurned source is destroyed.
Soul Chains A forge-spurned wields a soul chain: a cursed _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [spiked chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=60)_. Any other creature that has a _soul chain_ must succeed at a DC 22 Will save each day at sundown or transform into a forge-spurned permanently. A forge-spurned stops at nothing to retrieve its _soul chain_ if it’s lost.
name: Forge-Spurned

<traits>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Grick](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2182)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The wormlike gricks terrorize the caverns and tunnels in which it dwells, lying in wait near heavily traveled underground passages or subterranean cities for the chance to reach forth from the darkness and take its prey. Those laid low by a grick are rarely consumed on the spot. Instead, gricks haul back fresh food back to their lair in a tight burrow or high on a cavern ledge, where they consume their spoils in small bites at the gricks' leisure.

 The origin of gricks is unknown. Even though these creatures have a rudimentary intelligence, they don't have any social structure to speak of. Most gricks are encountered alone. Even on those occasions when unfortunate travelers meet multiple gricks in small groups, those gricks don't appear to communicate or work together; each instead attacks an individual target and retreats with its prize as soon as it manages to bring down an opponent. Capable predators, gricks also have a strangely weapon-resistant hide that makes them especially dangerous to the unprepared. Many novice adventurers have fallen to grick attacks merely because they couldn't damage these creatures with their non-magical weapons. Those familiar with gricks (especially [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1), [morlocks](/Monsters.aspx?ID=731), and [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102)) know the best strategy for dealing with them when one lacks access to magic weapons is to fall back and wait for more powerful or magical reinforcements.

 Gricks rely on their dark coloration and ability to climb walls to keep them out of sight until they're ready to spring an ambush. On occasions, when food becomes scarce in a particular area, gricks have been known to travel to the surface and roam the wilderness in search of prey, but they make these sojourns almost always out of necessity, lasting only as long as it takes them to find entrances to new subterranean lairs. These creatures prefer darkness and the comfort of a ceiling overhead; they avoid the open sky and go to great lengths to keep the cover of trees, low clouds, or buildings between them and the empty gulf overhead.

 Some Darklands societies have tried to recruit gricks as guardians or even beasts of burden, but their intelligence—while middling at best—is more than enough for them to stubbornly resist and even resent attempts at “training.” The best most societies can manage is to keep a grick guardian happy by making sure it remains well-fed and comfortable.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Will** +10 

</row>

**HP** 35

</row>

**Resistances**
physical 5 (except [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Gripping Tentacle** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature in the grick's reach uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The grick makes a tentacle Strike against the target. If the attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a move action, the grick disrupts that action.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +11,
**Damage** 1d12+6 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d8+6 bludgeoning

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Jungle Gricks</title>

These surface-adapted varieties of the underground grick appear in shades of green. They lurk in the undergrowth of jungles or climb low-hanging branches to drop on unsuspecting prey. Their greater skill at [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks applies in forested terrain instead of rocky terrain. Jungle gricks are heartier creatures than their underground-dwelling kin; all jungle gricks have the [elite creature adjustments](/Rules.aspx?ID=790) as a result.
</aside>
name: Grick

Not all oozes are mindless predators capable of little more than ambushing and consuming prey. The id ooze is a thinking, calculating creature with the capacity to carry out complex plots and plans—not always to secure a meal. Left to its own devices, an id ooze is just as content to spend decades or even centuries in introspective philosophical pursuits as it is to crawl through the underground rivers and moist caverns it prefers to call home. An id ooze can derive sustenance from the consumption of organic material, but without easy access to plants or animals to eat, they can subsist in a state of semi-hibernation for centuries while slowly absorbing residual psychic energies in locations that have been host to powerful emotions, such as long-standing temples that once hosted generations of worshippers, settlements that endured horrific wars or devastating disasters, or even just the home of a powerful occult spellcaster whose thoughts left marks upon the walls long after they moved on. An id ooze prefers water but is equally at home on land. The slimy creature appears as a patch of water until it attacks, when its gelatinous body piles up on itself to form a single pseudopod that strikes like an angry snake. **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Id Ooze](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2183) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 121 **Perception** +15; motion sense 60 feet, no vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 60 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Motion Sense** An id ooze can sense nearby creatures through vibration and air or water movement.
**Puddled Ambush** **Trigger** A creature enters or interacts with a space occupied by an id ooze; **Requirements** Initiative hasn't yet been rolled; **Effect** The id ooze automatically notices the creature, then makes a pseudopod Strike against one creature adjacent to itself before rolling initiative. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 105 **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), critical hits, precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Resistances** slashing 5, piercing 5 --- **Speed** 10 feet, climb 10 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** pseudopod +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d4+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid, DC 25 **Id Ooze Acid** An id ooze's acid damages only metal and organic materials, not stone. **Manipulate Ooze** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per turn; **Effect** The id ooze manipulates the senses and impulses within a [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) [ooze](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=79) of the id ooze's level or lower within 30 feet, issuing it telepathic orders to undertake a specific action. The orders must be simple, such as a directive to attack a specific target in preference to another, or to hold ground in a spot to protect a location, or even to adjust its positioning and body to form crude shapes or symbols. A manipulated ooze performs the direction to the best of its ability on its next turn, after which it returns to its normal behavior.
Brain Ooze Just as id oozes are an evolution of the lesser [gray ooze](/Monsters.aspx?ID=753), so do there exist evolutions of id oozes known as brain oozes. While brain oozes aren't much more powerful than an id ooze, they tend to be much crueler and more sadistic in their behavior. Brain oozes appear as disembodied brains that lash out with slimy tentacles. Where the id ooze possesses occult magic that allows them to confuse and hinder thought, brain oozes specialize in controlling thoughts and revels in the resulting feelings of superiority before, inevitably, they consume their mind-controlled victims.
name: Id Ooze

A tallow guardian typically appears as a vaguely humanoid-shaped mass of tallow or wax, its surface constantly melting, running down its body, and then hardening only to be reabsorbed into the central mass. The tallow guardian's face seems constantly on the verge of melting into a shapeless blob, yet the unsettling construct can still sense the world around it with ease. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Tallow Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2184) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 122 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +11 **Will** +13 **HP** 145 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5, physical 5 (except bludgeoning) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10 **Melt** If a tallow guardian takes any fire damage, its body melts somewhat and becomes even more difficult to damage. Until the end of its next turn, its resistance to slashing and piercing damage increases to 10, and its resistance to bludgeoning damage increases to 5.
**Slowed by Cold** If a tallow guardian takes any cold damage, it loses the benefits granted by melt and becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Encase** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) The tallow guardian attempts to pull a Medium or smaller creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) into its body. The tallow guardian attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check opposed by the grabbed creature's Reflex DC. If it succeeds, it encases the creature with its body. An encased creature is grabbed, is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), and must hold its breath or start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). It takes 2d6 fire damage from the tallow guardian's molten hot wax when it's first encased, and at the end of each of its turns while it's encased.
A tallow guardian can only have one encased creature at a time; if a tallow guardian encases a new creature, a previously encased creature is forced out of its body into an adjacent square of the creature's choice.
An encased creature can break free with a successful DC 22 check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). An encased creature can attack the tallow guardian, but only with [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks or with weapons of light Bulk or less. The tallow guardian is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the attack. If the tallow guardian takes 7 or more bludgeoning or slashing damage from an encased creature, the creature wrenches itself free. A creature that gets free by either method can immediately breathe and exits the tallow guardian's space, but it continues to take 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the molten wax that clings to its body.
If a tallow guardian is destroyed, any creature it has encased is automatically released as the guardian's body collapses into a shapeless mound of tallow. **Molten Wax** All [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) caused by a tallow guardian comes from globs of molten hot tallow that cling to its foes. This tallow cools automatically after 4 rounds, or immediately if the creature takes any cold damage; once the tallow cools, the persistent fire damage ends.
From Evil Intent While a tallow guardian itself isn't intrinsically evil, the traditional methods of creating it involve rendering humanoid corpses to generate the tallow needed to construct its frame. The guardian is then animated by siphoning fragments of souls that linger in the area into a rudimentary but mindless animating force. Other methods of infusing tallow guardians with raw positive energy or voluntary donations could work, but the traditional method persists, giving these constructs an unsettling legacy.
name: Tallow Guardian

Thoqquas are cantankerous creatures of fire and slag. Their bodies generate incredible heat that allows them to burrow or melt through most surfaces, even solid rock. Thoqquas are native to the vast ash deserts and lava fields of the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). There, they consume ore and minerals, which their furnace-like bodies then smelt into armor plates that grant the creatures their armored forms. These creatures' foremost body segment tapers into a straight, hornlike beak that glows with a blistering heat. Steam and smoke constantly hiss from their joints, and from a distance, thoqquas can be mistaken for strange metallic constructs. Adult thoqquas are 5 feet long and weigh 200 pounds. Thoqquas are dangerous to approach—they attack without thinking when startled or frustrated. If they don't immediately chase away intruders within their territory, then they might gradually come to view that area as property and even guard it. [Mephit](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=44) seem to understand this curious thoqqua thought process, occasionally brokering deals with the magma worms. The mephits claim that the mountain-sized ancestors of modern thoqquas were servants of the elemental lords and dug the first volcanoes in the young worlds of the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). These “progenitor worms” then retired to the worlds' cores, where their heat warms the planets even today. Thoqquas on the Material Plane do congregate around volcanoes—though whether this tendency is to protect a mineral-rich food site or sacred place, or simply out of comfort, depends on the thoqqua in question. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Thoqqua](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2185) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 123 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +7 **Will** +5 **HP** 45 **Immunities** bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 5 **Molten Body** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A thoqqua's body is hot enough to melt stone. A creature who grabs a thoqqua, is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by a thoqqua, or critically fails a melee Strike with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack or [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) weapon takes 1d4 fire damage (DC 15 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
**Reactive Thrash** **Trigger** A creature within the thoqqua's reach hits the thoqqua with a melee Strike; **Effect** The thoqqua instinctively thrashes into the attack, pressing its molten hot scales forward. The attacker must attempt a DC 15 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The attacker is unaffected.
**Success** The attacker takes 1d4 fire damage.
**Failure** The attacker takes 2d4 fire damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the weapon used to Strike the thoqqua becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) unless the weapon is made of metal, has more than 10 Hardness, or is resistant to or immune to fire. --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 15 feet **Melee** beak +9, **Damage** 1d8+3 piercing plus 1d6 fire and critical ignition **Critical Ignition** On a critical hit with a body Strike, a thoqqua inflicts an additional 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
Starving Thoqquas A thoqqua feeds on a combination of raw stone, ore, and heat. When denied these nourishments, a thoqqua won't perish from starvation, but it does grow less armored as it's forced to absorb and weaken its armored body plates. A starved thoqqua's Armor Class is reduced by 2, but it gains a +1 circumstance bonus on all attack rolls.
name: Thoqqua

Vargouilles are scavengers from the swamps of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12), where they flop and flap through strange and haunted skies in constant search of fresh souls to torment. In this daemonic nightmare realm, vargouilles play a role similar to that of a raven or vulture, although they augment these roles with a malevolent delight in causing pain and anguish that no scavenger bird could ever hope to match. A vargouille is larger than a human head, typically between 12 and 20 inches high with a 15- to 35-inch wingspan. While vargouilles aren't natives of the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), they can be found there nevertheless, occupying graveyards, ancient battlefields, or anywhere one can find remnants of death and decay. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 21 **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Vargouille](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2186) **Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 124, [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 35 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +4, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +10 **Will** +8 **HP** 28 --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** bite +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d8 piercing plus 1d6 poison **Vargouille's Kiss** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Requirements** The vargouille is adjacent to a [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing humanoid creature; **Effect** The vargouille nuzzles up against a helpless or willing humanoid creature in a parody of a lover's kiss—the creature must then attempt a saving throw or begin an unsettling transformation into a vargouille. The sickened, stupefied, and unconscious conditions from Vargouille's Kiss don't improve on their own until the disease is cured. This disease can't progress in stage as long as the victim is in sunlight or in the area of a 3rd-level or higher [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) effect. **Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude; **Stage 1** no ill effect (2 hours); **Stage 2** victim's hair falls out, [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (2 hours); **Stage 3** victim's ears elongate into winglike shapes and their teeth grow tangled and sharp, [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (2 hours); **Stage 4** as Stage 3, but the victim falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (2 hours); **Stage 5** death, and the victim's head transforms into a new vargouille that detaches from the body and flies off to spread evil on its own. **Vargouille's Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The vargouille distends its jaw awfully and emits an unnerving shriek. All creatures within 60 feet who can see and hear the vargouille must attempt a DC 15 Fortitude save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Vargouille's Shriek for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature is paralyzed. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save. **Tenacious Wounds** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature who takes poison damage from a vargouille must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save or be cursed. The cursed creature can't regain HP except via magic, and anyone Casting a Spell to heal the creature must succeed at a DC 18 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check or the healing has no effect. The vargouille's counteract level is equal to its creature level. A creature can attempt a new DC 18 Fortitude save once per day (typically when it makes its daily preparations) to end this curse. The curse ends automatically if the creature is healed to maximum HP.
Infestations of Evil While an evil spellcaster can certainly conjure vargouilles and seed them through a region to spread their kind, these minor fiends have an even more insidious method of crossing the planar boundary. In graveyards that have become corrupted by daemonic influences or cults devoted to the [Horsemen of the Apocalypse](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=11), fresh corpses can spontaneously serve as portals from [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12), allowing brand new vargouilles to be “born” from the heads of the dead.
name: Vargouille

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Weeping Jack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2187) **Source** [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 61 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Children of the Night** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The vampire's presence brings forth creatures of the night to do the master's bidding. These typically include [rat swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=347), [bat swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=45), and [wolves](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=101), but can include other creatures. The vampire can give telepathic orders to these creatures as long as they are within 100 feet, but they can't communicate back. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=60)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_, [_greater demon mask_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=430) --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +24 **Will** +24 **HP** 210 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** vampire **Mist Escape** **Trigger** The vampire is reduced to 0 HP. **Effect** The vampire uses Turn to Mist. It can take move actions to move toward its coffin even though it's at 0 HP. While at 0 HP in this form, the vampire is unaffected by further damage. It automatically returns to its corporeal form, unconscious, if it reaches its coffin or after 2 hours, whichever comes first. --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** _spiked chain_ +26 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire transforms into one of its animal forms or back into its normal form. Most vampires can turn into a bat, but some can turn into a different creature, such as a rat or a wolf. Use the options in the [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=4) and [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) spells as guidelines. Weeping Jack's bat form's fangs strike have a +25 attack bonus and deal 2d8+13 piercing damage.
  • **Bat** The vampire changes into a bat swarm or giant bat. It gains echolocation 40 feet, a land Speed of 20 feet and a fly Speed of 30 feet, and a fangs Strike with the same attack bonus as its highest melee attack. These fangs deal the same amount of damage as the vampire's claws, but deal piercing damage rather than slashing damage.
**Create Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by Drink Blood, the vampire can turn this victim into a vampire by donating some of its own blood to the victim and burying the victim in earth for 3 nights. If the new vampire is lower level than its creator, it is under the creator's control. If a vampire controls too many spawn at once (as determined by the GM), strong-willed spawn can free themselves by succeeding at a Will saving throw against the vampire's Will DC. **Dominate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The vampire can cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) at will as a divine innate spell (DC 29 Will save). Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that vampire's Dominate for 24 hours. Fully destroying the vampire ends the domination, but merely reducing the vampire to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell. **Dread Striker** Any creature that has the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition is also [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to Weeping Jack's attacks. **Drink Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the vampire's reach. **Effect** The vampire sinks its fangs into that creature to drink its blood. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and Weeping Jack regains 16 HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the vampire but increases the victim's drain value by 1. A vampire can also consume blood that's been emptied into a vessel for sustenance, but it gains no HP from doing so.
A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drain by 1 after 10 minutes. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Weeping Jack deals 3d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Turn to Mist** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vampire turns into a cloud of vapor, as the [_gaseous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) spell, or back to its normal form. The vampire loses fast healing while in gaseous form. The vampire can remain in this form indefinitely.
name: Weeping Jack

Armag was not born with that name, but today, he fully believes he is the legendary ancient leader reborn to lead the Tiger Lords back to an age of glory. He has long forgotten his birth name, as well as much of his childhood, as his brief but overwhelming recent experiences forced these memories out of his mind to make room for bloodlust, an addiction to violence, and obsession with mayhem. Only his worship of [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=7) remains today, yet even this has been corrupted—tainted by the fell influence of the Sisters of the Bloodshot Eye. Little remains in Armag's mind today save for a desire to conquer, to wield the mighty greatsword [_Ovinrbaane_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1748) in battle, and to revel in the glory of war. While he claims to be the reborn ancient hero, his “memories” of this previous life are largely hallucinations and fantasies implanted by the influence of his weapon. Armag himself appears as a muscular human with unruly brown hair, a full beard, and an expression that seems incapable of any other visage than anger, wrath, or fury. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Armag](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2188) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 592 **Perception** +24 **Languages** [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +24 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, [_Ovinrbaane_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1748) --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +23 **Will** +25 **HP** 300 **Resistances** physical 10 (weapons only) **No Time to Die** As long as Armag wields _Ovinrbaane_ and is raging, the combination of the sword's magic and Armag's furious conviction that he is the true Armag protects him from death. His eyes and the sword's blade weep with blood and glow with red light at this time. As long as these conditions persist, he never increases beyond [dying 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) and is not rendered [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). Instead, Armag becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) and continues to fight. If _Ovinrbaane_ is taken from him (such as via a successful [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41)), or if his rage ends while he is dying, he immediately dies regardless of his dying condition value. If he's healed to at least 1 Hit Point, the confusion effect immediately ends.
**Second Wind** **Trigger** Armag is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) and scores a critical hit with a melee strike or reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points with a melee Strike; **Effect** Armag draws upon inner reserves of furious energy. He is no longer fatigued.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _Ovinrbaane_ +29 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d12+13 slashing plus 1d6 bleed **Melee** shoulder spikes +27, **Damage** 3d6+13 piercing **Armag's Rage** **Requirements** Armag is not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) or raging. **Effect** Armag flies into a rage that lasts 1 minute. While raging, he gains a +6 status bonus to damage rolls and suffers a –1 status penalty to AC. During his rage, Armag gains an additional reaction at the start of his turn, but he can only use this additional reaction for Attacks of Opportunity. After the rage ends, Armag is fatigued. **Awesome Blow** **Requirements** Armag's last action was a successful Strike. **Effect** The weight of Armag's previous swing drives his foe back. He attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the target's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** Armag gains the critical success effects of a [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), then the critical success effect of a [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) against the target.
**Success** Armag gains the success effect of a Shove, then the success effect of a Trip against the target.
**Failure** Armag pushes the foe back 5 feet. **Greatsword Critical Specialization** On a critical hit from a greatsword Strike, the target is made off-balance and becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of Armag's next turn. **Shoulder Slam** Armag Strides forward and hunkers down to attempt a shoulder spike Strike against a target at the end of that movement. If he critically hits a creature with this Strike, that creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Armag

<title level="1" pfs="">[Castruccio Irovetti](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2189)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Since his flight from Numeria, Castruccio Irovetti has ruled Pitax for over half a decade, and in that time, he has firmly established his hold over this River Kingdom's political scene through a mix of cunning choice and luck. While he does have the support of the [nymph](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=77) [Nyrissa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2197), he hid from her the fact that he's managed to recover the magical sword she charged him with retrieving, making him loath to call upon her for aid. Furthermore, if he can defeat the PCs, surely that show of strength will bolster his reputation in her eyes, so that he can then present [_Briar_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1743) to her for an even greater reward. In Nyrissa, Irovetti sees the only candidate he could imagine taking on as his Queen.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 45

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/CastruccioIrovetti.png" />
</column>

</row>

[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +29

**Nyrissa's Favor** Nyrissa infused Irovetti with additional abilities. He gained darkvision and the ability to see invisible creatures as a result of this favor, in addition to the typical +2 status bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) when determining the effect of compositions.

**Items**
[_boots of speed_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=414), _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=144) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, [mindrender baton](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1753), palace key, [rod of razors](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1751), [_wand of dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [_wand of paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=213)

rod of razors +30 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d10+12 slashing

<actions string="Single Action" />
flechette +32 ([range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [volley 30 ft.](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 piercing

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 37, attack +30
- **Cantrips (8th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)
