- **2nd**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (3 slots)
- **3rd**
[Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=166), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) (3 slots)
- **4th**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Private Sanctum](/Spells.aspx?ID=235) (3 slots)
- **5th**
[Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) (3 slots)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (3 slots)
- **7th**
[Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=93), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) (3 slots)
- **8th**
[Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187), [Scintillating Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=267), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) (3 slots)

**Bard Composition Spells** 3 Focus Points
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Inspire Courage](/Spells.aspx?ID=386)
- **4th**
[Counter Performance](/Spells.aspx?ID=381), [Lingering Composition](/Spells.aspx?ID=389), [Inspire Heroics](/Spells.aspx?ID=388)

**Rituals** DC 37 inveigle

**Signature Spells**   Irovetti can heighten the following spells freely: [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [_duplicate foe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=93), [_private sanctum_](/Spells.aspx?ID=235), [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=291), and [_vampiric touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=354).

**Songstrike** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Irovetti sings out a short aria that resonates within his rod of razors, causing it to become infused with sonic energy. He attempts a Strike with the rod of razors, and if he hits, he inflicts an additional 5d10 sonic damage (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save)

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Darivan](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2190)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Darivan is not the classic image of a [Lamashtan](/Deities.aspx?ID=11) cultist. While deeply religious, his magical power stems not from his faith, but from a link to his ancestral past. The favor of a [dryad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=312) helped his grandfather escape from a brutal life and find a safe new homeland for his people, but Darivan never held patience for that. To Darivan, his sorcerous legacy and his sinister faith are two wholly separate elements, but his recent visions of the bloom of Lamashtu bringing a new swath of monsters to the Stolen Lands has compelled him strongly, showing him how his fey ancestry and his demonic faith can be made stronger.

 In combat, Darivan relies on his spells against ranged foes and his falchion and claw in melee. He activates Bloom Regeneration if an attack reduces him to 100 Hit Points or fewer, then shifts tactics to casting [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) and retreating upward to use spells against foes.

The man who would take the name Foras grew up millennia ago among worshipers of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), yet he came to believe that the Outer Gods were the keys to claiming personal power. He tricked his people's priests into to imparting eldritch truths they would not normally share, then shared them with a few fellow secretive scholars of the occult. Gathering those whom Foras could manipulate, he retreated to a new stronghold—a location now known as Candlemere. Here, Foras's cult gave up their sight and their souls, only to become trapped with him within their own compound when their leader's brother tracked them down and imprisoned them. Yet even this hasn't stymied Foras, and he continues to this day to work toward his goal of drawing [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=166) into the world. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Foras](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2191) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 595 **Perception** +33; unseen sight **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +34, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +37, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +33, [YogSothoth Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +37 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Unseen Sight** This is a precise sense that mimics [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) yet functions even if the eye sockets themselves are covered. More importantly, the unseen sight allows a Thresholder to see the truth of the world, granting them the ability to see things as they actually are as if they were under the effects of [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344), provided they are located within a region under the influence of YogSothoth. Currently in the Stolen Lands, this is limited to the island of Candlemere and the dungeons within it; beyond this area, the Thresholder cultists are [blind](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1). **Items** [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=166), _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)_, [_staff of power_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=361) --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +31 **Will** +35 resolve **HP** 355 **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 20 **Outside of Time** Foras exists outside of time; he doesn't age, doesn't need food or drink to survive, and can't be [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) unless he chooses to be
**Resolve** When Foras rolls a success on a Will save, he gets a critical success instead.

A living creature that enters the aura or ends its turn within it has some of its temporal essence drained away to Yog-Sothoth. It must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (slowed 2 on a critical failure). Worshipers of Yog-Sothoth are immune to Time Siphon. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _staff_ +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 3d4+10 bludgeoning **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 41, attack +36 - <title level="1" pfs="">[Grigori](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2192)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The bard Grigori is a charismatic orator and an agent of Pitax who seeks to undermine the PCs' authority while spying on their kingdom. He shows up in the kingdom's capital city while the PCs are out adventuring and immediately begins giving speeches criticizing and denigrating their leadership and actions. More and more of the townsfolk gather to hear him talk and, as the days wear on, he builds a wellspring of support among the disaffected settlers in the town. Every decision the PCs make has a winner and a loser, and Grigori is gifted at shifting focus to the losers in these decisions and riling them up. Whether it is appealing to the citizens who didn't get what they wanted when the PCs built a new city block, to spinning a narrative that paints a recent event in the most horrid light for those for whom the outcome was a negative, a properly played Grigori should become a constant barb in the players' side and one of the most memorable villains they encounter. Grigori takes great pains to avoid breaking any of the PCs' laws so that they have no legal justification to arrest him other than that they want to silence him.

 Grigori is more than just an itinerant storyteller; he's a spy and agent provocateur sent by [King Irovetti](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2189), the Lord of Pitax, to spread chaos and dissent in the town with the aim of overthrowing the PCs and possibly paving the way for Pitax to annex the settlement. In order to cover his tracks and retain plausible deniability, Irovetti used several layers of agents to hire Grigori; as far as the bard knows, he was hired to cause trouble in this fledgling town by agents from Fort Drelev to the west.

**Rhetorical Spell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) If the next action Grigori takes is to Cast a Spell, he attempts a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check against all observers' Perception DCs. On a success, he hides his spellcasting as part of his oratory, and observers don't realize he's Casting a Spell.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Hannis Drelev](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2193)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hannis Drelev spent his youth in Brevoy frustrated at the lack of opportunity for advancement. When he heard Lady Jamandi was looking for volunteers to aid her in exploring and taming the Stolen Lands, he saw an opportunity to bolster his fame. In the months since, Hannis has come to regret his decision, and now that he has been forced to surrender to [Irovetti](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2189) and [Armag](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2188), his attitude has shifted from frustration to outright cruelty. Drelev comes across as a sullen, greedy, conceited man, never satisfied with his lot in life, but always disdainful of anyone who has less than him. Though skilled at politics, the baron fancies himself a skilled swordsman as well. He loves dueling and often spars against his own guardsmen and senior officers. He likes to show off, taunting and belittling those he overpowers, but reacts angrily to situations where others get the best of him.

 A patient opponent who prides himself on his ability to skillfully outmaneuver his foes, Baron Drelev begins nearly every turn with Defensive Slice. He favors disarming foes, and attempts to do so once per round when facing an armed foe, mocking them wickedly if he's successful and cursing them violently if not.

Before his dreams were invaded and his ambitions enhanced by [Nyrissa's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2197) eldritch manipulations, Hargulka had already established himself as a leader among trollkind, though his goals never reached beyond bullying his fellow [trolls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=96) into rampaging the immediate environs for food and wealth to bring back to him as offerings. The notion of becoming a “king” never crossed his mind until he had a vivid, unforgettable dream of himself sitting atop a throne and basking in the fear and adoration of not only trolls, but [hobgoblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=13), [harpies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=258), and all manner of slavering monsters—creatures who might be viewed as dangerous enemies but, to Hargulka, were now his loyal minions. To Hargulka, the PCs' kingdom is rightfully his own. He intends to conquer them, claim their capital for himself, and dine on the flesh and bones of their citizens. Yet despite these violent goals, the troll knows better than to simply attack his enemies mindlessly. Instead, he spends his time working with his loyal trolls to seek out like-minded monsters in the wilds to recruit them to his cause. As a result, the longer Hargulka remains in power, the more dangerous and aggressive the monsters of the Stolen Lands will become! **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Hargulka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2194) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 598 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), _+1 [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376) [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) [morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=8)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 190 , regeneration 25 (deactivated by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Weaknesses** fire 15 **Dream of Rulership** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The lingering effects of Nyrissa's inspiration are stronger than any fear Hargulka faces. Whenever he attempts a saving throw against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect, he treats his actual result as one degree of success better than his rolled result.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _morningstar_ +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d8+9 piercing plus 1d6 sonic **Melee** jaws +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw **Royal Command** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101)) Hargulka issues an order to his followers. Each ally who hears and understands this command becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) until the end of that ally's next turn but can use the extra action only to Strike. Hargulka can't issue a Royal Command on his next turn. **Shield Rip and Chew** **Requirement** An opponent within reach has [Raised a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98). **Effect** Hargulka snatches at a foe's shield, pulling it out of the way. He attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the Reflex DC of an opponent who has Raised a Shield before making a jaws Strike against the opponent. If the Athletics check succeeds, the shield is not considered raised against the jaws Strike. On a critical success, Hargulka pulls the shield out of position, so it is no longer raised until the opponent Raises the Shield again. This counts as two attacks toward Hargulka's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the jaws Strike.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Jamandi Aldori](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2195)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Jamandi Aldori's upbringing in Restov could have ensured her a safe and relaxed life as one of that city's nobles, but her restlessness and frustration with “meddling” from the north increasingly pushed her away from the aristocratic lifestyle. She quickly mastered the art of the Aldori sword, and over the years has become one of Restov's most well-known swordlords, in part due to her skill in dueling, but of late as a result of her increasingly obvious pushes to expand Restov's power in directions that minimize reliance upon the rest of Brevoy. Beyond organizing the push into the Stolen Lands and her desire to foster a new ally to the south, she hopes some day to found additional dueling schools beyond Brevoy's borders where the Aldori style can be taught—a pursuit that not all of her peers equally approve of.

 Characters who earn Jamandi's trust and support can unlock access to the [Aldori duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=14) archetype at your option.

markdown:
[The Lantern King](/Deities.aspx?ID=95) is one of [the Eldest](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=7)—a pantheon of demigods whose rule over the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) is one of capriciousness that can be at times playful, enigmatic, bizarre, or even downright mean. For the most part, the Lantern King's plots and shenanigans tilt toward the cruel, and while he's long been a master at riding the proverbial razor's edge between mischief and outright sadism (if only by conserving his cruelest plans for those who invite that cruelty by being cruel themselves, as in the case of Nyrissa), his latest actions have certainly drawn the notice of the other Eldest. As such, they have largely distanced themselves from him, and as a result of this, any defeat the PCs deal to him in this adventure goes not only unpunished by the Lantern King's demigod siblings but will even be enforced, in a manner of speaking, by the other Eldest so that the Lantern King won't seek continued revenge against the PCs for their victories. Whether or not this also serves to teach the capricious eldest a lesson to temper his need for mischief with occasional bouts of patience and even mercy, only time can tell. The Lantern King's direct interactions with the PCs in this campaign are via an avatar—a powerful proxy created from himself to rule over the Lantern Kingdom. While this avatar isn't as powerful as the Lantern King himself, it remains the single most dangerous foe the party faces in Kingmaker. As a demigod, the Lantern King is an even more dangerous foe, equivalent in power to a 29th-level creature and possessing abilities beyond the scope of the core experience in the _Pathfinder Roleplaying Game_. Some of these abilities, such as the power to manipulate reality, to create powerful artifacts like the _Apology_, and to build entire demiplane realms like the Hunting Dream or the Lantern Kingdom, manifest during the course of the _Kingmaker Adventure Path_ not as rules content but as story elements the PCs will need to endure—see Chapter 11 of this book for more details. In combat, the Lantern King's avatar uses flight to remain out of melee for the first several rounds of combat, relying upon his spells to soften up the party before using his Strikes against them. He's fond of taking actions that disrupt, torment, or mock the PCs rather than those specifically tailored to maximize damage. For example, he often uses Playful Switch to swap a flying PC in the air with one who's on the ground and can't fly, or he uses illusions to create false foes or locations that play off of previous failures or embarrassments the PCs may have endured during the campaign itself. By playing the Lantern King's avatar in battle as more of a trickster than a cold-blooded killer, you'll not only enforce his legendary personality upon your players, but you'll also give their characters a bit more of a fighting chance against him in battle. He prefers to hold [_primal phenomenon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=231) in reserve to recover from an unwanted condition or situation, and as he realizes the PCs are dangerous foes, he abandons “playful tactics” for more serious or tactical decisions aimed toward defeating the party rather than toying with them. While the Lantern King faces no lasting inconvenience if his avatar is slain, the sting of such a defeat is enough to compel the avatar to back down and ask for a truce if the PCs reduce him to fewer than 75 Hit Points. If the PCs agree to a truce, the avatar compliments their prowess and promises to turn his attentions away from the Stolen Lands. Furthermore, he agrees to any additional terms the PCs ask for, provided they are within his ability to grant. This includes restoring Nyrissa's place and realm to the First World (although he cannot provide further information about her lost lover) and unmaking [_Briar_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1743) to restore her capacity to love. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 58 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 56 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 53 [Avatar of the Lantern King](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2196) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 600 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 300 feet, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +46, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +41, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +41, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +49, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +42, [First World Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +43, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +46, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +46, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +41, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +41, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +39, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +42, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +42 **Str** +9 **Dex** +12 **Con** +7 **Int** +9 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +12 --- **AC** 54 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +39 **Will** +41 **HP** 500 **Immunities** bleed, [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), [death](/Domains.aspx?ID=7) effects, [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 20 (except [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375)) **Weaknesses** cold iron 20, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 25 **Fortune's Friend** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** The Lantern King fails or critically fails a saving throw. **Effect** The result of the saving throw is improved one degree. The Lantern King can't Fortune's Friend for 1d4 rounds.
**Lantern King's Glow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 120 feet. The Lantern King illuminates his aura with bright light. At the start of his turn, this aura attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any ongoing [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) effect in the aura (counteract check +44). The Lantern King can suppress or activate this aura as a free action.
**Mocking Laughter** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. Whenever a creature misses the Lantern King with an attack or fails a skill check or saving throw, the creature is overtaken by overwhelming mirth at its mistake, as [_hideous laughter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=150) (DC 45 Will save). The creature is thereafter temporarily immune to mocking laughter for 1 hour.
**Reactive** The Lantern King gains an additional reaction at the start of his turn, but he can use this additional reaction only to take a reaction he hasn't already taken since the end of his previous turn.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** fly 60 feet **Melee** searing rune +44 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d6+24 bludgeoning plus 4d6 fire plus drain luck **Ranged** blinding beam +44 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+19 fire plus blindness **Primal Innate Spells** DC 48, attack +40 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233) (at will) - **8th** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=326), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) - **9th** [Disjunction](/Spells.aspx?ID=77), [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158), [Meteor Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=191) - **10th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Primal Phenomenon](/Spells.aspx?ID=231) - **Constant (6th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Rituals** DC 48 - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) - **6th** [Commune with Nature](/Rituals.aspx?ID=7), [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9), [Create Demiplane](/Rituals.aspx?ID=31), [Freedom](/Rituals.aspx?ID=11) - **9th** [Fantastic Facade](/Rituals.aspx?ID=32) **Adjust Temperature** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The Lantern King adjusts the temperature of his flames. He shifts from orange to blue, and his Strikes inflict cold damage until he takes this action again to switch back to fire damage. **Blindness** A creature damaged by the Lantern King's blinding beam Strike must make a DC 48 Fortitude save. If the Strike was a critical hit, the save's result is worsened one degree.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected by blindness.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the creature can attempt a DC 48 Fortitude save to end this blindness early.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded permanently. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The Lantern King can take on the appearance of any [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) or [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), but he typically does so only to trick and confuse creatures. He loses his searing rune and blinding beam Strikes, but he gains a melee fist Strike that inflicts 4d8+19 bludgeoning damage. **Drain Luck** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110)) When the Lantern King damages a creature with his searing rune Strike, he drains the creature's luck. The creature must succeed at a DC 48 Will save or gain a –1 status penalty to all checks; it is then temporarily immune to Drain Luck for 1 round. Further damage dealt by the Lantern King's searing rune Strikes increases the status penalty by 1 on a failed save to a maximum status penalty of –4. This status penalty is reduced by one every time the affected creature achieves a critical success on a die roll against a creature that is at least equal to them in level, but otherwise is permanent until removed. **Playful Switch** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The Lantern King targets two creatures within 60 feet, causing each to become surrounded by glowing runes. The Lantern King attempts a single [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check against each target's Reflex DC. The Lantern King can't use Playful Switch again for 1 round.
**Critical Success** If the Thievery check achieves a critical success against both targets, they swap places and are both stunned 1. Each creature must be able to fit into their new spaces or the effect fails.
**Success** If the Thievery check achieves a success against both targets (or a critical success on one and a success on another), the two targets swap places. Each creature must be able to fit into their new spaces or the effect fails.
**Failure** If the Thievery check fails against either target, neither target is affected.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but both creatures are temporarily immune to Playful Switch for 24 hours. **Prismatic Burst** **Trigger** A creature achieves a critical hit against the Lantern King or damages it with a [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) weapon. **Effect** The Lantern King casts [_prismatic spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=233).
name: Avatar of the Lantern King

summary_markdown: The Lantern King  is one of  the Eldest —a pantheon of demigods whose rule over the  First World  is one of capriciousness that can be at times …

markdown:
Nyrissa has not always been a power in the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), but when she fell in love with [Count Ranalc](/Deities.aspx?ID=92), one of the realm's [Eldest](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=7), she challenged her place in the plane's hierarchy. The lovers inspired each other to increasing acts of audacity, and it wasn't long before Nyrissa began to think of herself as the newest of the Eldest and proclaimed this to any who would listen, beginning to build her own nation in the First World. Another of the Eldest, [the Lantern King](/Deities.aspx?ID=95), saw opportunity for mayhem here. He spread his influence among his fellow demigods, and before long, several among the Eldest opposed Nyrissa's claim and took steps to challenge her. First, they sent a [jabberwock](/Monsters.aspx?ID=652) to destroy her, but Nyrissa defeated it and used its blood to create lesser jabberwocks under her own control. Then they attempted to rally a thousand powerful fey against her, but Nyrissa convinced those fey to serve her instead in return for a place in her domain. Finally, the Eldest exiled Count Ranalc to the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) for helping to cause the problem in the first place. Suddenly, Nyrissa found herself without an ally among the Eldest. Having expended much of her energy and resources to defeat the jabberwock and recruit the thousand, she knew that she had lost the war and surrendered, apologizing for her acts. The Lantern King decided that Nyrissa's apology would suffice only for the jabberwock's death; for her other deeds, he demanded a further punishment— Nyrissa's capacity to love. Without love, the Lantern King argued to the other Eldest, Nyrissa would not be able to sway another of them to her side. However, if she could replace the “thousand fey breaths” she stole from the First World with a thousand stolen nations, they would accept her apology for her other actions and restore her capacity to love. Faced with no real option, Nyrissa agreed. She was allowed to keep her domain, now known as Thousandbreaths, but it was carved off to form its own demiplane to serve as a sort of prison. Her love was taken from her and transformed into the sword [_Briar_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1743), which was then entrusted to the Lantern King for safekeeping. He gifted Nyrissa with a chalice he called the _Apology_, an artifact capable of transforming [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) kingdoms she conquered into motes of sand. Once the _Apology_ held a thousand motes of sand, those lands would be absorbed into the First World, Nyrissa would be forgiven, and love would be restored to her. The matter settled, the Eldest quickly moved on with their own inscrutable agendas. Yet while they eventually forget about what they'd done to Nyrissa, the nymph herself did not. She grew more and more obsessed with her loss of love—or perhaps it was the loss of love that caused her to grow more violent and obsessed. In her early attempts to discover the location of _Briar_, she received the visions that would come to haunt her—that _Briar_ would be returned to her, but only as an instrument of her own death. Now she has but one kingdom left to claim: the new kingdom risen in the Stolen Lands. With the War of the River Kings over and one kingdom a victor, the time is right for the final grain to be added to the _Apology_. That this will destroy a significant region on Golarion is irrelevant to the loveless nymph; in her obsession, she fails to see that this act may be the very thing that drives her enemies from to slay her as in the vision. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 56 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 54 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 51 [Nyrissa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2197) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 602 **Perception** +41; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +43, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +43, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +44, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +44, [First World Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +43, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +44, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +45, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +41, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +36 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +8 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +11 **Shame** Nyrissa is focused on her goal to conquer the final kingdom she promised the Lantern King, but she still suffers from shame (page 459) when she attacks anyone who wields _Briar_ or any character who rules a kingdom (particularly, any PC who serves as Ruler). Forgive Foe can be attempted against Nyrissa with a DC 51 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check. If Nyrissa is affected by a critical success from Forgive Foe, she calls for a truce to speak to the PCs as detailed on page 460.
**Wild Empathy** Nyrissa can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on and make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creatures. **Items** [_anklets of alacrity_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=397), [_major ring of fire resistance_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=455) <%END>, [_ring of spell turning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=458) --- **AC** 49 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +43 **Will** +41 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 495 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 20 **Blinding Beauty** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. Creatures that start their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 43 Will save or be permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) (plus [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) on a critical failure).
**Evasion** When Nyrissa rolls a success on a Reflex save, she gets a critical success instead.
**Protection from Decapitation** The first time Nyrissa is decapitated by any effect (including a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) weapon like _Briar_), the effect is negated. She takes no damage from the attack and is not decapitated, but she then loses her protection from decapitation for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** thorny lash +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d6+14 slashing plus 4d10 poison **Ranged** hurled thorn +41 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+14 piercing plus 2d10 poison **Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 46, attack +42 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333), [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157), [Meld into Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=188), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Shrink Item](/Spells.aspx?ID=285) (4 slots) - **4th** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211), [Private Sanctum](/Spells.aspx?ID=235), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) (4 slots) - **5th** [False Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=109), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) (4 slots) - **6th** [Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Flesh to Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=123), [Mislead](/Spells.aspx?ID=199), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) (4 slots) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237) (4 slots) - **8th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33), [Discern Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=75), [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) (4 slots) - **9th** [Contingency](/Spells.aspx?ID=49), [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Disjunction](/Spells.aspx?ID=77), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (4 slots; 1 already used to cast _contingency_) - **10th** [Wish](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) (1 slot) **Bloodline Spells** DC 46, 3 Focus Points - **10th** [Faerie Dust](/Spells.aspx?ID=508), [Fey Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=509), [Fey Glamour](/Spells.aspx?ID=510) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 46, attack +42 - **6th** [Restore Senses](/Spells.aspx?ID=259) (at will), [Tree Shape](/Spells.aspx?ID=342) - **7th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (×3) - **8th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×3), [Horrid Wilting](/Spells.aspx?ID=152), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) - **9th** [Nature's Enmity](/Spells.aspx?ID=205), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=248), [Tree Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=343) - **Constant (10th)** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=14) - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) - **6th** [Awaken Animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3), [Commune with Nature](/Rituals.aspx?ID=7), [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9), [Create Demiplane](/Rituals.aspx?ID=31) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Nymph queens can transform between their original form, which looks much like a typical nymph of their kind, and any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) form, typically choosing a more humanoid-looking version of their natural form.. **Contingency** **Trigger** Nyrissa is damaged by a melee strike; **Effect** [_Mislead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=199) activates on Nyrissa. **Focus Beauty** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) Nyrissa focuses her beauty upon a target within her blinding beauty aura. The creature takes 6d12 mental damage (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save) and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the end of their next turn (12d12 mental damage and permanent blindness on a critical failure). Nyrissa can Focus Beauty on a given creature only once per turn. **Inspiration** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Nyrissa can inspire an intelligent, willing creature by scratching that creature with a thorn from her body. This reduces that creature's Hit Points by 1 permanently, but grants the creature several abilities: [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), the ability to see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures, and an ability boost to an ability of Nyrissa's choice. A [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=3) granted this inspiration gains a +2 status bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) when determining the effect of compositions. Only one creature at a time can benefit from this inspiration. For the bulk of this Adventure Path, [King Irovetti](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2189) carries this inspiration, but once he is slain or defeated, who Nyrissa grants the bonus to instead (if anyone) is left to the GM to determine. **Quickened Casting** **Frequency** three times per day; **Requirement** Nyrissa hasn't used Quickened Casting since the start of her previous turn; Effect If Nyrissa's next action is to cast an arcane spontaneous spell that is 8th level or lower, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Switch Fables** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirement** Nyrissa is located in the twenty-foot diameter central area of one of the four Fables (areas M7a– M7d of the House at the Edge of Time); **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** Nyrissa Strides a minimum distance of ten feet, after which the reality of the demiplane folds around her in a tangle of vines and flowers. She then teleports from her current Fable to one of the other three of her choice, appearing in a square of her choosing within the central twenty feet of the chosen Fable.

<title level="1" pfs="">[The Stag Lord](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2198)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The Stag Lord never had a name, for his father Nugrah only called him “boy,” and then only when the old man demanded some sort of backbreaking chore or was looking for something to beat on. Times remained tough for the boy until the night he had the dream.

 A blindingly beautiful woman approached him, challenging him with a simple question: Why did he defer to his father when he himself deserved the honor of rule? The woman gave him a magical helm crafted from the skull of a stag, then promised him that taking command from his father would be but the first step toward commanding an army of his own.

 When he woke, he assumed his dream was just that, but then he saw that he still clutched the helm his nocturnal visitor had gifted him. So when his father, drunk and angry, came at him that afternoon, the boy fought back, beating his father to within an inch of death before setting out on his own into the wilderness.

 In the following years, the Stag Lord gathered outcasts and criminals to his side, commanding them with the same brutality he'd learned from his father. He also inherited a fondness for drink from his father, and today spends much of his time in a drunken haze.

**Perpetual Hangover** The Stag Lord is in a constant cycling state of being drunk or hungover. As a result, he is constantly [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and cannot reduce his sickened state to lower than 1 (although if effects increase his sickened value, he can reduce them back toward 1 as normal). The Stag Lord can willingly ingest food and drink (including potions and elixirs) while sickened. The values in this stat block have been adjusted to account for his sickened state.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
longsword +14 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d8+5 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_composite longbow_ +16 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 1d8+5 piercing

**Dread Striker**   The Stag Lord capitalizes on his enemies' fear; any creature that has the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition is also [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against his attacks.

**Hunt Prey** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The Stag Lord designates a single creature as his prey and focuses his attacks against that creature. He must be able to see or hear the prey, or he must be tracking the prey during exploration. He gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when he [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) his prey and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks when he [Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) his prey. The Stag Lord also ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within his second range increment against the prey he's hunting. The Stag Lord can have only one creature designated as his prey at a time. If he uses Hunt Prey against a creature when he already has a creature designated, the prior creature loses the designation and the new prey gains the designation. His designation lasts until his next daily preparations.

**Unfair Aim** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The Stag Lord lines up a shot and then attempts a ranged Strike against a hunted prey. On this strike, he treats the target as if they were [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16).

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The Stag Lord deals 2d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Tartuccio (Tartuk)](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2199)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Tartuccio likes to think of himself as having been hand-picked by [King Irovetti](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2189) of Pitax in his mission to claim the Stolen Lands for himself, but in fact King Irovetti has never met Tartuccio. The gnome grew up in Pitax and, of all that nation's rulers he'd observed, Irovetti impressed him the most with his admiration of art and style. That the king wasn't very supportive of art styles and pursuits he didn't enjoy never even registered in Tartuccio's mind, as his tastes were so similar.

 And so Tartuccio set into motion plans to catch the king's attention, so as to become first his student, then his trusted advisor, and in time his replacement. He even went as far as to seek out, research, and learn a potent magical technique that allowed him to change shape, intending to use this newfound supernatural power to adjust his appearance to be something more pleasing to the king's eye when the time came to petition to become his student. Tartuccio spent months scouring the rumor mills for opportunities, and when he heard of [Lady Jamandi's](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2195) call for heroes to expand into the Stolen Lands, he saw his chance. Tartuccio gathered his supplies and headed east.

 Once the events of Chapter 1 play out, Tartuccio is unsettled by the amount of competition he faces. He travels into the Stolen Lands where he's heard rumors of a [kobold](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=18) settlement that's ripe for the taking, uses his ability to change shape to assume the appearance of an unusual purple kobold, and work the Sootscales into a sort of private army he intends on using to wrest control of the PCs' nation just as they get started. His hopes of using the PCs' kingdom as a stepping-stone are threatened by the untimely conflict with the [mitflits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=245) of the Old Sycamore, as detailed in Chapter 2.

markdown:
At the height of his power well over 10,000 years ago, the original Vordakai was among the most powerful necromancers of the land. When this [cyclops](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=25) tyrant was defeated during the chaos following Earthfall, one of his apprentices claimed his name as well as his greatest treasure, the [_Oculus of Abaddon_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1747). Though he had been the least of the necromancer's apprentices, the new Vordakai was nevertheless a powerful wizard, but it would take more than power to claim the true Vordakai's mantle. Betrayed by followers of the original leader who refused to serve him, the new Vordakai was sealed into his own tomb, and over the passage of eons, the cyclops [lich](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=68) atrophied. Although much weakened today, Vordakai remains a dangerous opponent who can devastate a poorly prepared party on his own. Don't be afraid to play Vordakai as arrogant creature—he has existed for longer than most creatures on Golarion, after all, and if he makes arrogant mistakes (such as neglecting to cast spells defensively or provoking [Attacks of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)), that not only gives the PCs a chance to survive but also helps to establish the ancient lich as both overconfident and a bit careless from his long period of quiescence. Keep in mind that Vordakai is the most powerful foe the PCs have encountered in the _Kingmaker Adventure Path_ thus far, and in defeating him, they will accomplish something truly legendary. If Vordakai has the chance to prepare for battle before combat, he takes 3 rounds to cast [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) (choosing an energy type he knows the PCs favor as a result of watching them via his familiar), and then [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) just before entering battle. Finally, he activates [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) using the _Oculus of Abaddon_ before entering combat (doing so in the first round of a fight if instead he's caught by surprise by the PCs). Once combat begins, Vordakai starts by casting [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on the party's strongest healer; if successful, he commands the healer to his side to defend him by attacking anyone who attacks the lich in melee. On subsequent rounds, Vordakai uses ranged magic against the party, casting [_vampiric exsanguination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) as soon as he's reduced to 100 Hit Points or fewer. When facing only one or two foes, he prefers to attack with Consume Memories followed by a hand Strike, opting to save his spells for an emergency. The greatest diminishment of Vordakai's power is represented through the loss of his [_soul cage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545)—the magical item he initially used to trap his own soul and enable his transformation into a lich in the first place. He cannot create a replacement as long as he remains atrophied, but his arrogance works against him here. Only if he's reduced below 20 Hit Points does he realize that the PCs have a very good chance to defeat him, and if he survives long enough to take a turn after this realization, he casts his 5th-level [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) to flee to area C5 of his tomb. If he's able to escape in this way, he'll wait for a few days before returning to his tomb, infuriated if the PCs have released his prisoners and robbed him blind. At this point, Vordakai can become a recurring villain in your campaign! **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Vordakai](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2200) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 606 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Cyclops](/Languages.aspx?ID=30), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Abaddon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Items** [_Oculus of Abaddon_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1747), spellbook --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +20 **Will** +25 +1 status bonus vs. [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) **HP** 200 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, physical 10 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) bludgeoning) **Atrophied Lich** Sometimes, a lich that remains immobile and insensible for extended periods of time—as Vordakai did after he was imprisoned in his tomb just prior to Earthfall 10,000 years ago—can become atrophied. The exact effects of atrophy vary from lich to lich but always result in a reduction of spellcasting power and damage to their [_soul cage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545) link.
In Vordakai's case, he has forgotten much arcane lore and lost a significant amount of his spellcasting ability. At the height of his power, he was a 20th-level wizard, but today, his power has reduced to that of a 12th-level wizard. More troubling to the lich is the fact that he can no longer use a _soul cage_ to rejuvenate—if Vordakai is destroyed, he crumbles to dust, forever dead. Given time, Vordakai could recover his power and regain his rejuvenation ability, but it's unlikely the PCs will give him that chance, as it would take the lich several years to undo the ravages of centuries.
**Counterspell** **Trigger** A creature casts a spell Vordakai has prepared. **Effect** Vordakai expends a prepared spell to counter the triggering creature's casting of that same spell. He loses its spell slot as if he had cast the triggering spell. He then attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell.
**Flash of Insight** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per day. **Trigger** Vordakai is about to roll a d20. **Effect** Vordakai peers into an occluded spectrum of possible futures. He gets a success (but not a critical success) without rolling.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 29
**Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt Vordakai's spellcasting action, the lich attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** hand +24 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d8 negative plus paralyzing touch **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 35, attack +27 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) - **2nd** [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) - **3rd** [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) - **6th** [Planar Binding](/Rituals.aspx?ID=17) - **8th** [Imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13) (cannot currently cast) **Consume Memories** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Vordakai draws out a creature's memories and consumes them, learning bits and pieces of the world through the victim's thoughts. The lich makes a hand Strike against the target; if he hits, he deals 6d6 negative energy damage. The target must attempt a DC 32 Will save, after which it is temporarily immune to Consume Memories for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The target takes no additional ill effects.
**Success** The target becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target becomes stupefied 1 with an unlimited duration. Vordakai gains a +1 status bonus to all skill checks for 1 minute as a result of the consumed memories.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is stupefied 2 with an unlimited duration. In addition, Vordakai can telepathically learn one specific bit of secret information known by the victim, subject to the GM's discretion. **Drain Oculus** **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Vordakai taps into the _Oculus of Abaddon's_ power to cast any arcane spell up to 6th level, even if the spell being cast is not one of his prepared spells. **Familiar** Vordakai's familiar, Horagnamon, is on page 254. **Paralyzing Touch** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A creature damaged by the lich's hand Strike must succeed at a DC 32 Fortitude save. The creature becomes [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round on a failure. On a critical failure, the creature is paralyzed permanently, falls prone, and seems dead. A DC 25 Medicine check reveals the victim is alive.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Similar in form to the elk but far superior in size is the megaloceros. The average male is a towering creature with antlers that alone weigh nearly 100 pounds. These antlers are enormous and can be wider than their bodies are long. Megaloceroses stand up to 9 feet tall, weighing 1,500 pounds and with antlers growing over 12 feet wide.

<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Megaloceros Antlers</title>

The antlers of a megaloceros are some of the most impressive animal trophies, known to fetch a fair price among collectors, particularly those who live far from regions where these majestic elk live. A set of megaloceros antlers in collectible condition can fetch prices of 40 gp, but as with all animal trophies, peddling in such wares can attract the attention of angry druids, vengeful rangers, or offended naturalists.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Thylacine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2203)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A thylacine can be easily mistaken for a dog or jackal, but the defining stripes on their gold-hued backs and unusually wide gaping jaws negate this perception in close encounters. A thylacine is quite vocal, and uses a combination of growls, hisses, and gaping threat-yawns when threatened. An adult thylacine stands 2 feet tall at the shoulder, is 4 feet long (not counting an additional 2 feet of tail) and weighs roughly 50 pounds.The thylacine is a marsupial carnivore. This nocturnal predator typically hunts alone, only occasionally forming small packs. Because of their odd appearance, thylacines have a far harsher reputation than they probably should, playing a sort of bogeyman role in farming communities.

Primal bandersnatches are among the most powerful of their kind, due to their ties to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10). **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Primal Bandersnatch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2205) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 610 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (precise) 120 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +36, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +31 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +33 **Will** +31 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 395 ([fast healing 15](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15)) **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) **Planar Acclimation** The bandersnatch treats the plane it is on as its home plane.
**Confusing Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Quick Recovery** The bandersnatch recovers with frightening speed. At the end of its turn, it reduces the value of one debilitating condition it suffers (with the exception of [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11)) by 1. If it's [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17), or [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), it can instead succeed at a DC 16 flat check to end one of these conditions of its choice; it can't use quick recovery on other conditions that lack values.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 50 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing **Melee** claw +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing **Melee** tail +37 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d4+18 piercing plus pain **Ranged** quill +33 ([range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d4+18 piercing plus pain **Focus Gaze** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The bandersnatch fixes its gaze at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against the bandersnatch's confusing gaze. After attempting the save, the creature is temporarily immune to a bandersnatch's Confusing Gaze gaze until the start of the bandersnatch's next turn. **Frumious Charge** The bandersnatch Strides, ignoring difficult terrain, then makes two claw Strikes at the end of its movement. **Lash Out** At the start of its turn, the bandersnatch gains an additional reaction that can be used only to attempt an Attack of Opportunity. **Pain** A bandersnatch's quills create exceptionally painful wounds. The wounded creature must succeed at a DC 41 Fortitude save to resist becoming [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical failure) by this pain. Further bandersnatch Strikes that cause pain increase the amount of drain by 1 for each failed save to a maximum of drained 4. **Relentless Tracker** The bandersnatch can [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) while moving at its full speed.
Other Bandersnatches **Warning - this sidebar contains spoilers for the Kingmaker adventure path.** The bandersnatch presented here is a creature that retains its ties to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), known to some scholars as primal bandersnatches. Bandersnatches who have dwelt on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) for longer periods can be less dangerous, although it's unheard of for a bandersnatch to weaken below 13th level. Just as surely, even more powerful bandersnatches lie in wait in the deepest reaches of the First World.
name: Primal Bandersnatch

markdown:
A thick, green stalk with spiraling rows of thorns stands at the center of this enormous flowering plant. Each flower has a growing seed pod at its center, surrounded by vibrant petals in a wide variety of colors. Crafted after consecutive seasons of experimentation, prayer, and ritual, the bloom of [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=11) is the triumph of the cultist Darivan and his Cult of the Bloom. The fiendish plant aids their effort to infect the humanoid population of the Stolen Lands and replace them with monstrous predators. While the Bloom of Lamashtu is intelligent and is capable of understanding [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), it has very little interest in anything other than spreading its curse. Cultivated to spread misery and violence, the bloom is quietly malevolent, growing its seeds as rapidly as it can. It is 12 feet tall and weighs 600 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Bloom Of Lamashtu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2206) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 611 **Perception** +21; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (precise) 30 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); (cannot speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 **HP** 210 , regeneration 30 (deactivated by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** bludgeoning 10, piercing 10 **Weaknesses** good 10, slashing 10 **Defensive Burst** **Trigger** The bloom takes slashing damage. **Effect** The bloom of Lamashtu sprays a gout of acid against an adjacent creature, inflicting 9d6 acid damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The bloom can't use Defensive Burst for 1d4 rounds. --- **Speed** 5 feet **Melee** thorny vine +23 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing plus 2d6 poison **Ranged** thorn +19 ([range 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6 piercing plus 1d6 poison **Bloom Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A living creature is exposed to the bloom curse if it eats any portion of a bloom of Lamashtu, fails a save against the bloom's seed pod, or is critically hit by the bloom's thorny vine Strike. Unlike the bloom curse contracted from secondary sources (as detailed in Chapter 5), there is no onset time for this version of the curse, and the creature spawned by the victim's death may be much more powerful and is always chaotic evil.
**Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Stage 1** no ill effects (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 1 and 2d6 poison damage (8 hours); **Stage 4** unconscious and 4d6 poison damage (10 minutes); **Stage 5** death; **Special** A creature that dies from the bloom curse explodes in a blast of [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) foliage and releases a monster into the world. This creature is a chaotic evil low-intelligence [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20) or [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) (chosen by the GM ) of the same level as the dead creature. **Spew Seed Pod** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The bloom of Lamashtu's central stalk disgorges a pulsing seed pod from its flower, spitting it at a range of up to 60 feet and creating a 20-foot burst when the seed pod explodes into a spray of supernatural acidic poison. All creatures in this area must attempt a DC 29 Reflex save. The bloom of Lamashtu can't Spew Seed Pod for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 3d6 acid damage and 3d6 poison damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 5d6 acid damage and 5d6 poison damage and is exposed to the bloom curse.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but 10d6 acid damage and 10d6 poison damage, plus knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by the force of the blast.
Fiendish Genesis **Warning - this sidebar contains spoilers for the Kingmaker adventure path.** During this campaign, there is but one bloom of Lamashtu, as the cult's attempts to cultivate more of them have so far been failures. At your option, more of these fiendish plants could well sprout from the bodies of their victims in time, or they might even already exist in some overgrown corner of the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13). In this case, the bloom of Lamashtu becomes [Rare](/Traits.aspx?ID=137) rather than [Unique](/Traits.aspx?ID=161).
name: Bloom Of Lamashtu

The fetch chooses the type of weapon it manifests, but it does not gain any of that weapon's standard weapon traits, instead using the traits listed above in the fetch weapon Strike.

markdown:
In its true form, a vilderavn herald is a [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6)-sized raven. Its ability to assume a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) guise suits it to insinuate itself into the retinue of a mortal ruler or to travel without too much concern, but more often these deadly fey directly serve more powerful rulers, such as [the Eldest](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=371). **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Vilderavn Herald](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2210) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 614 **Perception** +35; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Heraldry Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +33, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +36, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +33 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +36 **Will** +33 **HP** 375 **Immunities** [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 20 **Aura of Disquietude** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet, DC 35. As [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17), plus a creature [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) by the aura becomes suspicious: it doesn't count any other creature as its ally, and it can't [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) or flank. On a critical failure, the creature also can't be a willing target for harmless or helpful magic. The vilderavn can activate or deactivate this aura using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** greatsword +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d12+16 slashing plus bloodbird **Melee** jaws +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d8+16 piercing plus bloodbird **Melee** talon +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d8+16 slashing plus bloodbird **Occult Innate Spells** DC 41 - **5th** [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57) (at will), [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will) - **8th** [Ethereal Jaunt](/Spells.aspx?ID=105), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **Constant (6th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Bloodbird** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature hit by a vilderavn's melee attack becomes cursed, taking 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that's difficult to stanch: the DC to stop the bleeding using [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) is 35, and healing the creature to full HP doesn't automatically end the bleeding. Removing the curse ends the bleeding. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vilderavn takes on the shape of a [wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=415) or [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=416), a wolf-raven hybrid, or its raven knight form: a Small or Medium humanoid in black full plate carrying a greatsword. The vilderavn can use its jaws attack only in a form that has a wolf's head, its talon attack only in a form that has raven talons, and its greatsword only in raven knight form. **Souleater** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the vilderavn kills a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) target with a critical hit using its jaws Strike, it rips out and devours the target's heart and soul as part of the attack. While the target is dead, the vilderavn can Change Shape into the target's form, gaining a +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) the target. If magic would resurrect the creature, the caster must succeed at a DC 38 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check to extract the target's soul from the vilderavn; otherwise the spell fails.
Other Vilderavns **Warning - this sidebar contains spoilers for the Kingmaker adventure path.** The vilderavn heralds in Kingmaker serve the Lantern King, and are more powerful than others of their kind. The typical [vilderavn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1360) is a 16th-level monster.
name: Vilderavn Herald

markdown:
Whimwyrms are unpredictable dragons native to the whimsically dangerous [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10). While their coloration and appearance might evoke reactions of adoration, their moods shift quickly from one extreme to the other. Many who have assumed that their adorability and relatively small size equates to safety have met swift and grisly ends on a whimwyrm's fangs or magic. A whimwyrm is a slender, sleek dragon, not much longer than seven feet from snout to tail, but its speed in combat is unnerving. When a whimwyrm attacks, be it with its fanged mouth or its long tail, it makes several quick, darting snaps or lashes at once. While stylistically, these attacks consist of multiple bites or lashings, they still resolve as a single Strike. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Whimwyrm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2211) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 615 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), fog vision, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +30, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +29 **Str** +5 **Dex** +9 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 **Fog Vision** Fog and mist doesn't impair a whimwyrm's vision; it ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from fog and mist. --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +34 **Will** +29 **HP** 280 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 15 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Fangs only. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** frenzied fangs +35 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d12+12 piercing **Melee** whiplashing tail +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+12 slashing **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **7th** [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **8th** [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [Scintillating Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=267) - **9th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=93) **Mind-Numbing Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The whimwyrm exhales a 40-foot cone of sparkling magenta mist. Creatures within the area must attempt a DC 38 Will save as their minds are overwhelmed by a sudden riot of false (and typically unpleasant) memories, taking 10d12 mental damage depending on their save. The whimwyrm can't use mind-numbing breath for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature becomes outlined in sparkling dust, negating any [invisibility](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) it has in effect for 1 round and takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage, and its invisibility is negated for 2 rounds.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 minute, and its invisibility is negated for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round, dazzled for 1 minute, and its invisibility is negated for 1 minute. **Meddling Tail** **Trigger** The whimwyrm damages a creature with its whiplashing tail Strike; **Effect** The whimwyrm can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) the damaged creature.
Insult Challenges When a whimwyrm's insults result in combat, it rolls [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) for initiative. A PC who engaged in the insults can roll Performance as well, and if they insulted the whimwyrm, it begins the combat [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) as it sputters in indignation if the Performance check also exceeds the Whimwyrm's Will DC of 39.
name: Whimwyrm
