Wild hunt hounds are intelligent hunters and expert trackers that take the lead against elusive prey. Left to their own devices, they seek out creatures that are notoriously difficult to track. They are always surrounded with green mist composed of fragments of the spirits of the wild hunt's victims, a mystic fog that forms itself into a spectral pack to fight at its side. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Wild Hunt Hound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2212) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 616 **Perception** +29; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), greensight, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +27 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Greensight** The wild hunt hound ignores the concealed condition from leaves and other foliage.
**Planar Acclimation** The wild hunt hound treats the plane it is located on as its home plane.
**Wild Hunt Link** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) 500 feet. A wild hunt hound grants all other members of the wild hunt in the aura except wild hunt hounds the effects of its constant [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) innate spell. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +24 **Will** +25 **HP** 300 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) 15 **Instinctive Cooperation** The wild hunt hound automatically achieves a critical success on saving throws against effects created by other allied [wild hunt](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=323) creatures.
**Wild Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. Those who meet the gaze of a wild hunt hound come face-to-face with the hunt's fearful power and find it difficult to act in the face of such a legendary legacy. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must succeed at a DC 31 Will save to resist becoming [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (slowed 2 on a critical failure). The wild hunt hound can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. --- **Speed** 50 feet **Melee** jaws +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d10+11 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) - **5th** [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) (×3) - **6th** [Stone Tell](/Spells.aspx?ID=310) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) - **Constant (7th)** [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The wild hunt hound fixes its glare at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against the wild hunt hound's wild gaze. If the creature was already [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) by the wild gaze before attempting its save, a failed save causes it to be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round. After attempting its save, the creature is temporarily immune to Focus Gaze from any wild hunt until the start of the wild hunt hound's next turn. **Summon Pack** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** With a howl, the wild hunt hound summons a fog that manifests into several misty hounds. These foggy hounds shift and race and howl in a 20-foot aura as long as the wild hunt hound Sustains the effect. While the hounds seem to be made of fog, their teeth are very real, and any creature that ends its turn in this aura takes 6d6 piercing damage from numerous bites (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
name: Wild Hunt Hound

**Instinctive Cooperation** The wild hunt horse automatically achieves a critical success on saving throws against effects created by other allied wild hunt creatures.

**Wild Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Bewildering Hoofbeats for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is distracted by the sound. Until the start of the creature's next turn, if it attempts to use a reaction, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the reaction is disrupted, wasted with no effect.
**Failure** The creature is bewildered by the sound, with the same effects as success except it is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the confusion effect persists for 1 minute or until the creature recovers by other means. **Deafening Cry** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The wild hunt horse gives out a thundering cry in a 15-foot cone. These lithe, androgynous humanoids have pointed ears, glowing green eyes, and hair that flows softly at all times as if in a gentle breeze. They are methodical hunters who fire their bows with uncanny accuracy. Since wild hunt archers tend to be slower on foot than most of their companions, they often rely on [wild hunt horses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2213) to provide increased mobility. Rather than carrying normal arrows, they can produce arrows as needed from magical quivers—these quivers do not function in this manner for anyone other than wild hunt archers. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Wild Hunt Archer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2214) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 618 **Perception** +30; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), greensight, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Greensight** The wild hunt archer ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from leaves and other foliage.

**Wild Hunt Link** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) 500 feet. A wild hunt archer grants all other members of the wild hunt in the aura except wild hunt archer the ability to target [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) creatures with ranged Strikes without having to succeed at a DC 5 flat check. **Items** _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_, living bow --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +32 **Will** +28 **HP** 340 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) 15 **Instinctive Cooperation** The wild hunt archer automatically achieves a critical success on saving throws against effects created by other allied wild hunt creatures.

They relish the chance to seek revenge upon those who have wronged or insulted the wild hunt, but they tend to grow bored when the stakes aren't lethal. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Wild Hunt Scout](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2215) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 619 **Perception** +34; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), greensight, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +37, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +34 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 **Greensight** The wild hunt scout ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from leaves and other foliage.

If the beam hits, it inflicts no damage (nor does it inflict precision damage from a sneak attack), but it overstimulates the target's mind, causing them to become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) and [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round. On a critical hit, the target is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round. **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The wild hunt scout fixes its glare at a creature it can see within 30 feet. These objects vanish upon the wild hunt scout's death, or as soon as they no longer possess the items. **Deny Advantage** A wild hunt scout isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), or flanking creatures of your level or lower, or creatures of their level or lower using surprise attack. However, they can still help their allies flank. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A wild hunt scout's Strikes deal 3d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

markdown:
Each wild hunt follows a wild hunt monarch, who travels between the realm of the fey and the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) in search of new quarry. These limber and regal humanoids have elk antlers, long pointed ears, flowing white hair, and luminescent green eyes. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Wild Hunt Monarch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2216) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 620 **Perception** +35; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), greensight, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +36, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +36, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +37, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +37 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +10 **Greensight** The wild hunt monarch ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from leaves and other foliage, mist, and fog.
**Planar Acclimation** The wild hunt monarch treats the plane it is located on as its home plane.
**Wild Hunt Link** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) 1 mile. A wild hunt monarch grants all other members of the wild hunt in the aura except wild hunt monarchs an increasesed range of wild hunt link to affect all wild hunt members within a mile. Other wild hunt creatures in this link who end their turn within 60 feet of a wild hunt monarch gain [regeneration 50](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=26) until the end of their next turn; this regeneration can only be deactivated by forcing the wild hunt out of the 60-foot aura or by killing the wild hunt monarch. **Items** [_monarch's glaive_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20), monarch's horn --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 **HP** 450 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 20 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Instinctive Cooperation** The wild hunt monarch automatically achieves a critical success on saving throws against effects created by other allied wild hunt creatures.
**Wild Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. Those who meet the gaze of a wild hunt monarch come face-to-face with the hunt's fearful power and find it difficult to act in the face of such a legendary legacy. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must succeed at a DC 39 Will save to resist becoming [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (slowed 2 on a critical failure). The wild hunt monarch can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** glaive +38 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+20 slashing plus 1d6 sonic and 1d6 [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** horns +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 4d8+20 piercing plus brutal gore **Primal Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) - **6th** [Stone Tell](/Spells.aspx?ID=310) - **7th** [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Wind Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=376) - **9th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116) - **10th** [Primal Phenomenon](/Spells.aspx?ID=231) - **Constant (10th)** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Rituals** DC 44 - **6th** [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) (creatures with the [wild hunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=435) trait only) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9) **Brutal Gore** A creature critically hit by a monarch's horns strike is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). **Call Glaive or [three-actions]** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The wild hunt monarch wields a magic weapon known as a _monarch's glaive_. This weapon functions as a _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ in the monarch's hands only. The monarch can call their glaive to their hands using this action, transporting it across any distance. If their glaive is destroyed, the monarch can create a new one by using Call Glaive as a three-action activity that gains the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. **Call to the Hunt** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The wild hunt monarch blows their monarch's horn, causing two [wild hunt scouts](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2215) or four [wild hunt archers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2214) part of the monarch's wild hunt link and no more than 1 mile from the monarch's location to appear as the monarch chooses at any point within 30 feet. They act immediately before the wild hunt monarch in initiative starting on the next round. **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The wild hunt monarch fixes its glare at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against the wild hunt monarch's wild gaze. If the creature was already [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) by the wild gaze before attempting its save, a failed save causes it to be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round. After attempting its save, the creature is temporarily immune to Focus Gaze from any wild hunt until the start of the wild hunt monarch's next turn. **Wild Reincarnation** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** The wild hunt monarch kills an adjacent non-[fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) creature. **Effect** The wild hunt monarch attempts to reincarnate the creature as a fey. The slain creature must attempt a DC 42 Fortitude save, and it takes a –2 status penalty to this save if it was under any [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) effect placed by the monarch when it died.
**Critical Success** The creature is instead restored to life as if via [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243) (heightened to 10th level), and it is permanently immune to Wild Reincarnation from that wild hunt monarch.
**Success** The creature is not reincarnated and remains dead.
**Failure** The creature is temporarily reincarnated as a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) creature of the monarch's choice. The new fey must be of a level equal to or less than the dead creature's level, and it functions in all ways as that fey creature; it retains none of the abilities and only scatterings of the memories or personality of the reincarnated character, and it follows the commands and orders of the wild hunt monarch. After 1 minute, the reincarnated fey dies and its body reverts back to its original corpse, which can then be restored to life normally.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the reincarnated fey remains alive until slain; a [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196), [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) or similarly powerful spell or effect can reverse the reincarnation as well.
name: Wild Hunt Monarch

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Gromog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2218) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 28 **Perception** +1; [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9 **Str** +5 **Dex** -1 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -2 **Blinded** Gromog's eyes have been wounded by Linzi. In time, he'll recover his eyesight (mostly), but for the duration of this combat he is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1).
**Itchy** Not only have the itchy lice in Gromog's furs reduced his AC, Reflex save, and attack modifier through distraction (resulting in the values below the standard values for an ogre warrior), he also must succeed at a DC 10 flat check at the start of each round to avoid spending 2 actions furiously scratching himself. On a successful flat check, he must spend only one action scratching at the vermin. Finally, as a side effect of the discomfort caused by his lice, Gromog is too distracted to properly wield his ogre hook. While the weapon can still inflict grievous damage, as long as he remains itchy it doesn't possess the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait these weapons normally possess. **Items** lice-infested furs, [Ogre Hook](/Weapons.aspx?ID=86) --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +4 **Will** +5 **HP** 28 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** ogre hook +9 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d10+3 piercing
name: Gromog

<row gap="medium">

(currently 25)

</row>

**Wounded Arm** Jurgrindor's left arm is dreadfully wounded, courtesy of Amiri's greatsword. The discomfort of this wound causes Jurgrindor to take a –2 circumstance penalty to his Armor Class and Strikes (these modifications are included in his stats). At the end of each of his turns, Jurgrindor must succeed at a DC 12 flat check or the wound begins to bleed again, dealing him 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). He can't use [Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7) or [Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38) (as typical giants) until he's healed back to full hit points.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning

**Chill Breath** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) Jurgrindor breathes out a 15-foot cone of freezing moisture that quickly condenses into ice, dealing 2d6 cold damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that critically fails the save is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 18). Jurgrindor can't use Chill Breath for 1d4 rounds.

</column>
name: Jurgrindor

**Overhand Smash** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** Volodmyra is wielding her greataxe and is adjacent to an enemy; **Effect** Volodmyra raises her greataxe above her head and puts all of her might into a single attack. If the attack hits, Volodmyra deals an additional 8 points of damage and the opponent must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude save to avoid being knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**Spinning Sweep** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** Volodmyra successfully Strikes a second enemy when making a sweep attack with her axe and is within 10 feet of a third enemy she hasn't yet attacked; **Effect** Volodmyra spins in a circle (Stepping 5 feet if she chooses) and makes a third attack against a foe she hasn't attacked this round. This third attack suffers only a –8 penalty to the attack roll (instead of the standard –10 on a third Strike). If it hits, Volodmyra deals an additional 2d6 points of damage from the furious impact.

</column>

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Davik Nettles](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2226) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 67 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [ranseur](/Weapons.aspx?ID=35)_ --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 70 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 7 **Fearsome Gaze** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 18 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes frightened 2.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If Davik is reduced to 0 Hit Points, his bones and gear melt into water that runs back into the river or seeps into the ground, leaving nothing behind. The next night, he rises again from his original location in the rubble located downstream from the ruined ferry crossing. When he does, he is completely healed of all damage, but now his unholy anger is directed at those who previously destroyed him as well as at [the Stag Lord](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2198). He can use [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) to threaten the one who destroyed him, promising to drag them to a drowning death unless the Stag Lord's remains are given to the river. [_Dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (DC 19 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check) can remove this sinister link between Davik and his destroyer, preventing Davik from using nightmare on that creature again until they return to this location. As long as the PCs don't abandon their goal of hunting the Stag Lord, Davik remains content and does not plague their dreams.
**Rotting Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 10 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) on a critical failure for as long as it's sickened). While within the aura, creatures also take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against fear and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute and does not take the penalty to saves against fear effects. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** _ranseur_ +14 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10+7 piercing **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **4th** [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) - **Constant (2nd)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) (self only) **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) Davik fixes his glare at a creature he can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against Davik's Fearsome Gaze. On a failed save, if the target is already [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), the value of its frightened condition is increased by 1 (or by 2 on a critical failure). After attempting its save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of Davik's next turn. **Sure Possession** As long as Davik Nettles continues to exist, his _+1 ranseur_ cannot be taken from him. If disarmed of the _+1 ranseur_, if he drops it, or if it's destroyed, the ranseur melts into water; he can retrieve his _+1 ranseur_ by pulling it out of any body of water as an Interact action. If Davik is put to rest permanently by fulfilling his quest, his _+1 ranseur_ loses this ability and can be claimed by someone else.
name: Davik Nettles
